contract.title: Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (California)
contract.preamble: This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (this “Agreement”) is entered into and effective as of January 15, 2025 (the “Effective Date”), by and between TechStart Inc., with Alice Johnson serving as CEO (“TechStart”), and InnovateCo LLC, with Bob Smith serving as CTO (“InnovateCo”). TechStart and InnovateCo may each be referred to herein as a “Party,” and collectively as the “Parties.”

clauses.title: Definitions
clauses.content:
1.1 “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by or on behalf of a Party (the “Disclosing Party”) to the other Party (the “Receiving Party”), whether disclosed before or after the Effective Date, in any form (oral, visual, written, electronic, or other), that (a) is designated as confidential or proprietary, or (b) reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure. Confidential Information includes, without limitation: business plans; product roadmaps; inventions; research; models; algorithms; source code; object code; software designs; system architecture; APIs; technical specifications; datasets; training/validation data (including data schemas and labeling methodologies); analytics methods; pricing; marketing strategies; customer and supplier information; financial information; security procedures; and information regarding the existence and status of discussions relating to the Purpose.

1.2 “Representatives” means a Party’s and its affiliates’ directors, officers, employees, contractors, consultants, attorneys, accountants, financial advisors, and other professional advisors who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those set forth in this Agreement.

1.3 “Affiliates” means, with respect to a Party, any entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with such Party, where “control” means the power to direct management or policies through ownership of voting securities or otherwise.

clauses.title: Purpose; Permitted Use
clauses.content:
2.1 The Receiving Party shall use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent.

2.2 The Receiving Party shall not use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to compete unfairly, to circumvent the Disclosing Party in relation to identified opportunities disclosed for the Purpose, or to develop or improve products or services outside the Purpose, except to the extent such use is based solely on the Receiving Party’s Independent Information (as defined in Section 5.1) and not on the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.

clauses.title: Non-Disclosure; Standard of Care
clauses.content:
3.1 The Receiving Party shall (a) keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information confidential, (b) not disclose such Confidential Information to any third party except to Representatives as permitted under this Agreement, and (c) protect such Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care the Receiving Party uses to protect its own confidential information of like importance, but in no event less than a reasonable standard of care.

3.2 The Receiving Party is responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.

3.3 The Receiving Party shall not issue any press release or public statement regarding the Purpose or the Parties’ discussions without the other Party’s prior written consent, except as required by law.

clauses.title: Limited Disclosure to Representatives; Need-to-Know
clauses.content: The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information only to those of its Representatives who (a) have a bona fide need to know such Confidential Information for the Purpose, and (b) are bound by written or professional obligations of confidentiality and restricted use at least as protective as those contained herein. Upon the Disclosing Party’s request, the Receiving Party shall provide written confirmation that its Representatives with access are subject to such obligations.

clauses.title: Exclusions from Confidential Information
clauses.content:
5.1 Confidential Information does not include information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by competent written records: (a) is or becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Representatives; (b) was lawfully known by the Receiving Party without restriction on use or disclosure prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party (“Independent Information”); (c) is rightfully received by the Receiving Party from a third party without breach of any obligation of confidentiality owed to the Disclosing Party; or (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of or reference to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.

5.2 The combination of features will not be deemed within the exclusions above merely because individual components are publicly available, if the combination itself is not publicly available and was disclosed as Confidential Information.

clauses.title: Compelled Disclosure
clauses.content: If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or valid legal process (including subpoena or court order) to disclose any Confidential Information, the Receiving Party shall, to the extent legally permitted: (a) promptly notify the Disclosing Party in writing so the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy; (b) reasonably cooperate (at the Disclosing Party’s expense) with the Disclosing Party’s efforts to obtain such protection; and (c) disclose only that portion of Confidential Information that the Receiving Party is legally required to disclose and use reasonable efforts to ensure confidential treatment for any disclosed Confidential Information.

clauses.title: Return or Destruction of Confidential Information
clauses.content:
7.1 Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party shall promptly (and in any event within thirty (30) days) return or destroy all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in the Receiving Party’s possession or control, including all copies, summaries, and extracts.

7.2 Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain (a) one archival copy of Confidential Information solely for legal, regulatory, or dispute-resolution purposes, and/or (b) Confidential Information that is automatically stored in routine system backups, provided that such retained information remains subject to this Agreement and is not accessed except as permitted herein.

7.3 Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing its compliance with this Section 7.

clauses.title: Term; Confidentiality Period; Survival
clauses.content:
8.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until terminated by either Party upon thirty (30) days’ written notice to the other Party.

8.2 The Receiving Party’s obligations of non-disclosure and restricted use with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term shall continue for three (3) years from the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information.

8.3 Notwithstanding Section 8.2, to the extent any Confidential Information constitutes a trade secret under applicable law (including the California Uniform Trade Secrets Act (Civil Code §§ 3426 et seq.) and/or the federal Defend Trade Secrets Act), the Receiving Party’s obligations shall continue for so long as such information remains a trade secret under applicable law.

clauses.title: No License; Reservation of Rights
clauses.content: All Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. No license or other rights are granted to the Receiving Party under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, or other intellectual property rights of the Disclosing Party by virtue of this Agreement or any disclosure hereunder, except the limited right to use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose as expressly set forth herein. All rights not expressly granted are reserved.

clauses.title: No Obligation to Proceed; No Warranty
clauses.content:
10.1 Nothing in this Agreement obligates either Party to proceed with any transaction, partnership, or other relationship, and each Party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to terminate discussions at any time.

10.2 All Confidential Information is provided “AS IS.” The Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties, express or implied, regarding the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information, except that nothing in this Agreement limits liability for fraud or willful misrepresentation to the extent such limitation is not permitted by law.

clauses.title: Mutual Non-Solicitation (California-Compliant)
clauses.content:
11.1 For a period of twelve (12) months following the Effective Date, neither Party shall knowingly solicit for employment (or engagement as an independent contractor) any person who is, at the time of solicitation, an employee of the other Party and who (a) was directly involved in the discussions or activities under the Purpose, or (b) was identified to the soliciting Party as being involved in the Purpose; provided, however, that this Section 11 shall not restrict (i) general solicitations not specifically targeted at such employees (including postings on public job boards or general advertisements), or (ii) hiring of any person who responds to such general solicitations without targeted outreach.

11.2 This Section 11 is intended to be interpreted and enforced only to the extent permissible under applicable California law, including California Business and Professions Code § 16600 and related case law. The Parties acknowledge that California law may restrict certain non-solicitation provisions; accordingly, the Parties agree that this Section 11 shall not be construed to prohibit lawful competition or employee mobility, and shall apply only to targeted solicitation as described herein.

11.3 This Section 11 does not restrict either Party from soliciting or doing business with customers, vendors, or other commercial counterparties of the other Party, except to the extent such activity would require use or disclosure of the other Party’s Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement.

clauses.title: Equitable Relief
clauses.content: The Parties agree that any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages may be an insufficient remedy. Accordingly, in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity, the non-breaching Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief (without the necessity of posting a bond to the extent permitted by law) to prevent or curtail any such breach or threatened breach.

clauses.title: Limitation on Non-Compete; No Restriction on Lawful Competition
clauses.content: The Parties acknowledge and agree that this Agreement does not impose any non-competition obligation. The Parties further agree that nothing in this Agreement shall be interpreted to restrict any Party from engaging in lawful business activities or competition, provided that such Party does not use or disclose the other Party’s Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement.

clauses.title: Governing Law; Venue; Jurisdiction
clauses.content: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflict of laws principles. The Parties agree that any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state courts located in Santa Clara County, California, or, if federal jurisdiction exists, the United States District Court for the Northern District of California, and each Party irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts.

clauses.title: Notices
clauses.content:
All notices and other communications under this Agreement must be in writing and will be deemed given: (a) when delivered personally; (b) when sent by confirmed email (with a copy sent by another method set forth herein upon request); (c) one (1) business day after being sent by a nationally recognized overnight courier; or (d) three (3) business days after being mailed by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, in each case to the Parties at their respective addresses set forth below (or to such other address as a Party may designate by notice).

If to TechStart:
TechStart Inc.
Attn: Alice Johnson, CEO
[Address]
Email: [Email]

If to InnovateCo:
InnovateCo LLC
Attn: Bob Smith, CTO
[Address]
Email: [Email]

clauses.title: Assignment
clauses.content: Neither Party may assign or transfer this Agreement, by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that either Party may assign this Agreement without consent to an Affiliate or in connection with a merger, acquisition, reorganization, or sale of all or substantially all assets, provided that the assignee agrees in writing to be bound by this Agreement and the assigning Party remains responsible for obligations arising prior to assignment.

clauses.title: Severability; Reformation
clauses.content: If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect. To the extent permitted by law, the Parties agree that any invalid or unenforceable provision shall be reformed and enforced to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid and enforceable while preserving the Parties’ intent.

clauses.title: Entire Agreement; Amendments; Waivers
clauses.content: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous understandings and agreements, whether written or oral, relating to such subject matter. Any amendment or modification of this Agreement must be in writing and signed by both Parties. No waiver of any provision shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the waiving Party. No waiver shall constitute a waiver of any other provision or any subsequent breach.

clauses.title: Counterparts; Electronic Signatures
clauses.content: This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which will be deemed an original and all of which together constitute one instrument. Signatures delivered by electronic means (including PDF, DocuSign, or other electronic signature platforms) shall be deemed effective and binding to the fullest extent permitted under applicable law, including the U.S. Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act (E-SIGN) and the California Uniform Electronic Transactions Act (Civil Code §§ 1633.1 et seq.).

clauses.title: Authority
clauses.content: Each Party represents and warrants that (a) it has the full power and authority to enter into this Agreement and perform its obligations hereunder, and (b) the individual signing on its behalf has been duly authorized to do so.

contract.title: One-Way Non-Disclosure Agreement (California)
contract.preamble: This One-Way Non-Disclosure Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of January 15, 2025 (the "Effective Date"), by and between Stealth Startup Inc., a company with its principal place of business in California, on behalf of itself and its Founder, Sarah Chen ("Disclosing Party"), and Venture Capital Partners, on behalf of itself and its Investment Associate, Michael Brown ("Receiving Party"). Disclosing Party and Receiving Party may be referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

clauses.title: Definitions
clauses.content:
1.1 "Confidential Information" means any non-public information disclosed or made available by or on behalf of Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, whether before or after the Effective Date, in any form (oral, written, electronic, visual, or otherwise), that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure. Confidential Information includes, without limitation: business plans; product plans; technical data; inventions; designs; software (including source code and object code); trade secrets; know-how; research and development; customer and supplier information; marketing information; financial information; pricing; fundraising and financing information; cap table information; and the fact that discussions are taking place regarding the Purpose and their status.

1.2 "Representatives" means Receiving Party’s and its affiliates’ directors, officers, employees, partners, members, managers, agents, attorneys, accountants, advisors, and potential financing sources or co-investors, in each case who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those set forth in this Agreement.

1.3 "Trade Secret" has the meaning set forth under applicable law, including the California Uniform Trade Secrets Act (Cal. Civ. Code §§ 3426 et seq.) and the federal Defend Trade Secrets Act (18 U.S.C. § 1836).

clauses.title: Permitted Purpose and Use Restrictions
clauses.content:
2.1 Purpose. Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose.

2.2 Use Restrictions. Receiving Party shall not (a) use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Purpose; (b) exploit the Confidential Information for its own benefit or for the benefit of any third party (except as necessary for the Purpose through Representatives); or (c) reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to derive the composition, underlying ideas, structure, or source code of any prototypes, software, models, or other tangible embodiments of Confidential Information, except to the extent such restriction is prohibited by applicable law.

2.3 No License. Except for the limited right to use Confidential Information for the Purpose, nothing in this Agreement grants Receiving Party any rights, by license or otherwise, in or to any Confidential Information, including any patent, copyright, trademark, mask work, trade secret, or other intellectual property right of Disclosing Party.

clauses.title: Non-Disclosure; Standard of Care
clauses.content:
3.1 Non-Disclosure. Receiving Party shall keep the Confidential Information strictly confidential and shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity except to its Representatives in accordance with this Agreement.

3.2 Standard of Care. Receiving Party shall protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information of like importance, but in no event less than reasonable care.

3.3 Responsibility for Representatives. Receiving Party is responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives. Receiving Party shall ensure that each Representative who receives Confidential Information is informed of the confidential nature of the information and is bound to protect it in a manner consistent with this Agreement.

clauses.title: Exclusions from Confidential Information
clauses.content: Confidential Information does not include information that Receiving Party can demonstrate by competent written records: (a) is or becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party or its Representatives; (b) was lawfully known by Receiving Party without restriction on use or disclosure prior to disclosure by Disclosing Party; (c) is lawfully received from a third party without breach of any obligation of confidentiality owed to Disclosing Party; or (d) is independently developed by Receiving Party without use of or reference to the Confidential Information.

clauses.title: Compelled Disclosure
clauses.content: If Receiving Party or any of its Representatives is required by applicable law, regulation, or valid legal process (including subpoena or court order) to disclose any Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall, to the extent legally permitted, (a) provide Disclosing Party with prompt written notice of such requirement so that Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, and (b) reasonably cooperate, at Disclosing Party’s expense, with Disclosing Party’s efforts to obtain confidential treatment. If disclosure is required notwithstanding Disclosing Party’s efforts, Receiving Party may disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed and shall use reasonable efforts to ensure that such disclosed Confidential Information is treated confidentially.

clauses.title: Term; Confidentiality Period; Survival
clauses.content:
6.1 Term. This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until terminated by either Party upon written notice to the other Party.

6.2 Confidentiality Period. Receiving Party’s obligations of non-disclosure and restricted use under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information shall continue for two (2) years from the date of each disclosure.

6.3 Trade Secrets. Notwithstanding Section 6.2, Receiving Party’s obligations with respect to any information that constitutes a Trade Secret shall continue for so long as such information remains a Trade Secret under applicable law.

6.4 Survival. Sections that by their nature should survive termination (including, without limitation, Sections 2 through 13) shall survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement.

clauses.title: Return or Destruction of Confidential Information
clauses.content: Upon Disclosing Party’s written request, Receiving Party shall promptly (and in any event within ten (10) business days) return or destroy all Confidential Information in Receiving Party’s possession or control, including all copies, summaries, analyses, and extracts. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may retain one (1) archival copy of Confidential Information solely for legal, regulatory, or compliance purposes, or as automatically retained in accordance with bona fide backup and document retention policies, provided that any retained Confidential Information remains subject to this Agreement and is not accessed except as required for such purposes.

clauses.title: No Obligation to Disclose; No Reliance; No Warranty
clauses.content:
8.1 No Obligation to Disclose. Disclosing Party has no obligation to disclose any particular information, and the Parties may discontinue discussions related to the Purpose at any time.

8.2 No Warranty. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS". DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, REGARDING THE ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.

8.3 No Reliance. Receiving Party acknowledges that it is responsible for conducting its own evaluation and analysis for the Purpose and is not relying on Disclosing Party to provide all information that may be material to Receiving Party’s decision-making.

clauses.title: Equitable Relief
clauses.content: Receiving Party acknowledges that unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages may be an insufficient remedy. Accordingly, in addition to any other rights and remedies available at law or in equity, Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to prevent or curtail any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, without the necessity of posting bond except as required by law.

clauses.title: No Non-Compete; No Restriction on Lawful Competition
clauses.content: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to, nor shall it be interpreted to, prohibit Receiving Party from engaging in any lawful business, trade, or profession, or to restrain lawful competition. The Parties acknowledge that, under California Business and Professions Code § 16600, contractual provisions that restrain a person from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business are generally void, subject to limited statutory exceptions not intended to apply here.

clauses.title: No Employment, Agency, or Partnership; No Independent Contractor Representation
clauses.content: The Parties are independent entities. Nothing in this Agreement creates any partnership, joint venture, fiduciary relationship, employment relationship, agency, or other similar relationship between the Parties. Neither Party has authority to bind the other Party. The Parties acknowledge that this Agreement concerns confidentiality obligations only and does not purport to classify any worker as an independent contractor or employee for purposes of California law (including AB 5 and the ABC test).

clauses.title: Assignment
clauses.content: Receiving Party may not assign or transfer this Agreement, in whole or in part, whether by operation of law or otherwise, without Disclosing Party’s prior written consent. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Section is null and void. Disclosing Party may assign this Agreement to any successor in interest by merger, consolidation, reorganization, or sale of substantially all of its assets, provided that such successor agrees in writing to be bound by this Agreement.

clauses.title: Notices
clauses.content: All notices required or permitted under this Agreement must be in writing and will be deemed given when (a) delivered personally, (b) sent by confirmed email (with a copy sent by a nationally recognized overnight courier), (c) delivered by a nationally recognized overnight courier, or (d) mailed by certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, in each case to the Parties at their respective addresses on file with the other Party or to such other address as a Party may designate by notice. Email notices shall be sent to email addresses designated by the Parties in writing.

clauses.title: Governing Law; Venue; Severability
clauses.content:
14.1 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of laws principles.

14.2 Venue. The Parties agree that any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in San Francisco County, California, and each Party irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction of such courts and waives any objection based on inconvenient forum.

14.3 Severability. If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect, and the invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision shall be enforced to the maximum extent permitted by law consistent with the Parties’ intent.

clauses.title: Entire Agreement; Amendments; Waiver; Counterparts; Electronic Signatures
clauses.content:
15.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements, understandings, and communications, whether written or oral, relating to such subject matter.

15.2 Amendments. Any amendment or modification of this Agreement must be in writing and signed by both Parties.

15.3 Waiver. No waiver of any breach of any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any prior, concurrent, or subsequent breach. No waiver shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the Party against whom the waiver is sought to be enforced.

15.4 Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which is deemed an original, and all of which together constitute one instrument.

15.5 Electronic Signatures. Signatures transmitted electronically (including via PDF or a commercially recognized e-signature service) are deemed to be original signatures and shall be enforceable to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, including the U.S. Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act and the California Uniform Electronic Transactions Act.

contract.title: California Independent Contractor Services Agreement
contract.preamble: This Independent Contractor Services Agreement (this “Agreement”) is entered into as of 2025-02-01 (the “Effective Date”) by and between AppDev Corp (the “Client”), through its Product Manager, David Lee, and Emma Wilson, Senior Developer (the “Contractor”). Client and Contractor may be referred to herein individually as a “Party” and collectively as the “Parties.”
contract.recitals:
WHEREAS, Client desires to retain Contractor to perform certain software development services on a project basis; and
WHEREAS, Contractor represents that Contractor has the experience, skill, and resources to perform such services; and
WHEREAS, the Parties intend that Contractor will perform as an independent contractor and not as an employee, and intend this Agreement to be interpreted consistent with applicable California law.
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises contained herein, the Parties agree as follows:

clauses.title: Engagement; Scope of Services
clauses.content:
1.1 Engagement. Client hereby engages Contractor, and Contractor accepts such engagement, to perform the services described in this Agreement (the “Services”).

1.2 Services Description. Contractor shall provide full-stack development for a mobile application, including (i) React Native frontend development, (ii) Node.js backend development, and (iii) AWS integration, along with reasonable related tasks customarily necessary to deliver such work, as requested by Client from time to time and agreed by Contractor.

1.3 Deliverables. Contractor shall provide source code, documentation, configurations, and other work product created in connection with the Services (collectively, “Deliverables”).

1.4 Changes. Any material change to scope, schedule, or assumptions shall be documented in a written change order or written amendment signed by both Parties (email acceptable), including any fee adjustments, if applicable.

clauses.title: Term
clauses.content:
2.1 Term. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated pursuant to this Agreement, shall continue through 2025-07-31 (the “End Date”).

2.2 Survival. Provisions that by their nature should survive termination or expiration (including confidentiality, intellectual property, payment obligations accrued, indemnity, limitation of liability, and dispute resolution) shall survive.

clauses.title: Fees; Invoicing; Payment
clauses.content:
3.1 Fees. Client shall pay Contractor a fee of USD $12,000 per month during the Term for performance of the Services (“Fees”), unless otherwise agreed in a written change order.

3.2 Invoicing. Contractor shall invoice Client monthly in arrears (unless otherwise agreed in writing), itemizing Services performed and any approved expenses.

3.3 Payment Terms. Client shall pay undisputed invoiced amounts within thirty (30) days after receipt of invoice.

3.4 Disputed Amounts. Client shall notify Contractor in writing of any good-faith dispute regarding an invoice within ten (10) days after receipt, specifying the basis for the dispute. The Parties shall work in good faith to resolve disputes promptly. Client shall timely pay the undisputed portion.

3.5 Method of Payment. Payment shall be made by check, ACH, wire, or other method reasonably acceptable to Client.

3.6 Late Payments. Any undisputed amounts not paid when due may accrue interest at the lesser of (i) 1.0% per month or (ii) the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, from the due date until paid.

clauses.title: Expenses
clauses.content:
4.1 Ordinary Expenses. Unless expressly approved in advance in writing by Client, Contractor shall be responsible for all costs and expenses incurred in performing the Services.

4.2 Approved Expenses. Client shall reimburse Contractor for reasonable, pre-approved, out-of-pocket expenses directly related to the Services within thirty (30) days after receipt of appropriate documentation.

clauses.title: Independent Contractor Relationship; No Employment Benefits
clauses.content:
5.1 Independent Contractor. Contractor is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, joint venturer, partner, or fiduciary of Client. Contractor shall have no authority to bind Client to any contract or obligation without Client’s prior written consent.

5.2 Taxes and Withholdings. Contractor is solely responsible for all federal, state, and local taxes, withholdings, reporting, and other obligations arising from the Fees paid under this Agreement, including without limitation income taxes, self-employment taxes, unemployment insurance contributions, and any other payroll-related taxes or assessments. Client will not withhold taxes from payments to Contractor, except as required by law.

5.3 Benefits. Contractor is not eligible for, and hereby waives any claim to, any of Client’s benefits, including but not limited to workers’ compensation coverage (except as required by law), unemployment insurance benefits, disability insurance, paid leave, retirement benefits, or health insurance.

5.4 Control of Work. Subject to Client’s requirements regarding the desired results and Deliverables, Contractor shall determine the manner, means, location, and hours of performing the Services.

5.5 AB 5 / Classification. The Parties intend that the relationship be properly classified under applicable California law, including the ABC test and any applicable exemptions (Labor Code § 2775 et seq.). The Parties agree to cooperate in good faith to structure performance consistent with lawful independent contractor status; however, nothing herein is intended to waive or limit any nonwaivable rights under applicable law.

clauses.title: Tools, Equipment, and Access
clauses.content:
6.1 Contractor Equipment. Contractor shall supply Contractor’s own tools, equipment, and internet access necessary to perform the Services, except as otherwise agreed in writing.

6.2 Client Systems Access. To the extent Contractor requires access to Client systems, repositories, cloud accounts, or facilities, such access is granted solely for performance of the Services, is revocable at any time, and shall be subject to Client’s security and acceptable-use policies as provided to Contractor.

6.3 Return of Property. Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon Client’s request, Contractor shall promptly return or securely delete (as directed by Client) all Client property and Client Confidential Information in Contractor’s possession or control, except to the extent retention is required by law.

clauses.title: Confidentiality
clauses.content:
7.1 Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by or on behalf of Client to Contractor, whether in oral, written, electronic, visual, or other form, including without limitation business, technical, product, roadmap, financial, customer, pricing, security, and personnel information, and any notes or materials derived therefrom.

7.2 Obligations. Contractor shall (i) use Confidential Information solely to perform the Services, (ii) not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without Client’s prior written consent, and (iii) protect Confidential Information using at least reasonable care and no less than the care Contractor uses to protect its own confidential information of similar importance.

7.3 Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that Contractor can demonstrate by competent evidence: (i) is or becomes publicly available through no breach by Contractor; (ii) was rightfully known by Contractor prior to disclosure; (iii) is rightfully received from a third party without breach of any duty of confidentiality; or (iv) is independently developed by Contractor without use of or reference to Client’s Confidential Information.

7.4 Compelled Disclosure. If Contractor is required by law, regulation, or court order to disclose Confidential Information, Contractor shall (to the extent legally permitted) provide Client with prompt written notice and reasonably cooperate with Client’s efforts to seek protective treatment. Contractor shall disclose only the portion legally required.

7.5 Duration. Contractor’s obligations under this Section shall continue during the Term and for three (3) years thereafter; provided that trade secrets (as defined under applicable law) shall be protected for so long as they remain trade secrets.

7.6 Protected Disclosures; No Restriction on Rights. Nothing in this Agreement prohibits Contractor from reporting possible violations of law to any government agency or entity, making other disclosures protected under whistleblower provisions of law or regulation, or discussing wages, hours, or working conditions as protected by law. Contractor is not required to notify Client of any such reports or disclosures. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to limit any rights under California Civil Code § 47, California Code of Civil Procedure § 2017.310, or the federal Defend Trade Secrets Act (18 U.S.C. § 1833(b)).

clauses.title: Intellectual Property; Work Made for Hire; Assignment
clauses.content:
8.1 Definitions. “Work Product” means all Deliverables and any inventions, discoveries, improvements, works of authorship, designs, developments, processes, techniques, code (source and object), documentation, and other materials created, conceived, reduced to practice, or otherwise developed by Contractor (alone or with others) in connection with performing the Services.

8.2 Work Made for Hire; Assignment. To the fullest extent permitted by law, Work Product that qualifies as a “work made for hire” shall be deemed made for hire for Client. To the extent any Work Product does not qualify as a work made for hire, Contractor hereby irrevocably assigns and agrees to assign to Client all right, title, and interest worldwide in and to such Work Product, including all intellectual property and proprietary rights therein.

8.3 Further Assurances. Contractor shall execute and deliver such documents and take such further actions as Client may reasonably request to evidence, perfect, maintain, or enforce Client’s rights in the Work Product, including patent, copyright, and other filings. Client shall reimburse Contractor for reasonable out-of-pocket costs incurred at Client’s request in providing such assistance.

8.4 Moral Rights. To the extent permitted by applicable law, Contractor hereby waives and agrees not to assert any “moral rights” or similar rights in the Work Product.

8.5 Contractor Background Technology. If Contractor incorporates into the Work Product any pre-existing tools, code, libraries, frameworks, know-how, or materials owned or controlled by Contractor (collectively, “Contractor Background Technology”), Contractor grants to Client a perpetual, worldwide, irrevocable, transferable (as part of a transfer of the Work Product or Client’s business), sublicensable, royalty-free license to use, reproduce, modify, distribute, perform, display, make, have made, sell, offer for sale, import, and otherwise exploit such Contractor Background Technology solely as incorporated in or necessary to use the Work Product.

8.6 Open Source. Contractor shall not incorporate open-source software into Deliverables in a manner that would require disclosure of Client source code or impose “copyleft” or similar reciprocal obligations on Client (including GPL, AGPL, or similar licenses), unless Client provides prior written approval identifying the applicable license and intended use.

8.7 California Labor Code § 2870 Notice. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement does not require assignment of any invention that qualifies fully under California Labor Code § 2870 (and related statutes), including an invention developed entirely on Contractor’s own time without using Client’s equipment, supplies, facilities, or trade secret information, except for inventions that either (a) relate at the time of conception or reduction to practice to Client’s business, or actual or demonstrably anticipated research or development, or (b) result from any work performed by Contractor for Client.

clauses.title: Representations and Warranties
clauses.content:
9.1 Mutual Authority. Each Party represents and warrants that: (i) it has full power and authority to enter into and perform this Agreement; and (ii) the execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement does not violate any agreement to which it is a party.

9.2 Contractor Warranties. Contractor represents and warrants that: (i) Services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with generally accepted industry standards; (ii) Contractor will comply with applicable laws in performing the Services; (iii) to Contractor’s knowledge, the Work Product as delivered will not knowingly include malicious code intended to damage or disrupt systems (e.g., viruses, worms, time bombs); and (iv) Contractor will not knowingly misappropriate any third-party intellectual property in the Work Product.

9.3 Disclaimer. Except as expressly stated in this Agreement, neither Party makes any other warranties, express or implied, including any implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement.

clauses.title: Indemnification
clauses.content:
10.1 Contractor Indemnity. Contractor shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client and its officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any third-party claims, damages, liabilities, penalties, fines, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of or relating to: (i) bodily injury, death, or tangible property damage to the extent caused by Contractor’s gross negligence or willful misconduct; (ii) Contractor’s material breach of this Agreement; or (iii) allegations that the Work Product, as delivered by Contractor (excluding Client materials and specifications supplied by Client), infringes a third party’s intellectual property rights.

10.2 Client Indemnity. Client shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Contractor from and against any third-party claims, damages, liabilities, penalties, fines, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of or relating to Client materials or instructions provided by Client to the extent they infringe a third party’s intellectual property rights.

10.3 Indemnification Procedures. The indemnified Party shall promptly notify the indemnifying Party of any claim (provided that failure to provide prompt notice will relieve the indemnifying Party only to the extent materially prejudiced), permit the indemnifying Party to control the defense and settlement, and reasonably cooperate at the indemnifying Party’s expense. The indemnifying Party shall not settle any claim in a manner that admits fault or imposes non-monetary obligations on the indemnified Party without the indemnified Party’s prior written consent (not to be unreasonably withheld).

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses.content:
11.1 Limitation. To the maximum extent permitted by law, except for Excluded Claims (defined below), in no event shall either Party be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or for any loss of profits, revenue, data, or business opportunity, arising out of or related to this Agreement, whether in contract, tort, or otherwise, even if advised of the possibility of such damages.

11.2 Cap. To the maximum extent permitted by law, except for Excluded Claims, each Party’s total cumulative liability arising out of or related to this Agreement shall not exceed the total Fees paid or payable by Client to Contractor under this Agreement in the three (3) months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

11.3 Excluded Claims. “Excluded Claims” means: (i) a Party’s breach of confidentiality obligations; (ii) Contractor’s infringement indemnity obligations under Section 10.1(iii); (iii) a Party’s gross negligence or willful misconduct; and (iv) amounts owed by Client for Services properly performed and invoiced.

clauses.title: Termination
clauses.content:
12.1 Termination for Convenience. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon fourteen (14) days’ prior written notice to the other Party.

12.2 Termination for Cause. Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other Party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within ten (10) days after receiving written notice of breach specifying the nature of the breach.

12.3 Effect of Termination. Upon termination: (i) Contractor shall promptly cease performing the Services (except as needed for orderly transition as requested by Client); (ii) Contractor shall deliver to Client all completed and in-progress Work Product and Deliverables paid for or otherwise due to Client under this Agreement; and (iii) Client shall pay Contractor for Services performed through the effective date of termination and any approved, unreimbursed expenses, subject to Client’s right to dispute invoices in good faith.

12.4 No Early Termination Fee. Except as expressly stated in this Agreement or a signed change order, neither Party shall owe an early termination fee or liquidated damages solely due to termination.

clauses.title: Non-Solicitation; No Non-Compete
clauses.content:
13.1 No Non-Compete. The Parties acknowledge that, except for statutory exceptions, non-compete provisions are generally unenforceable in California (including California Business and Professions Code § 16600). Nothing in this Agreement is intended to restrict Contractor from engaging in any lawful profession, trade, or business.

13.2 Optional Non-Solicitation (Subject to Law). During the Term and for twelve (12) months thereafter, Contractor shall not solicit for employment any employee of Client with whom Contractor had material contact through performance of the Services; provided, however, that this Section shall be construed narrowly to the maximum extent enforceable under applicable California law, and shall not prohibit general solicitations not targeted at Client employees.

clauses.title: Insurance
clauses.content:
14.1 Coverage. Upon Client’s reasonable request, Contractor shall maintain commercially reasonable insurance coverage appropriate for the Services, which may include general liability and professional liability (errors and omissions) coverage.

14.2 Proof of Insurance. Upon request, Contractor shall provide a certificate of insurance evidencing required coverage.

clauses.title: Notices
clauses.content:
15.1 Notices. All notices under this Agreement must be in writing and will be deemed given when delivered by personal delivery, by nationally recognized overnight courier, or by email with confirmation of transmission (or other evidence of sending) to the addresses or email addresses designated by each Party.

15.2 Notice Information. The Parties shall provide their notice addresses and email addresses to each other in writing. Either Party may update its notice information by notice to the other Party in accordance with this Section.

clauses.title: Governing Law; Venue; Dispute Resolution
clauses.content:
16.1 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflict of law principles.

16.2 Venue. Subject to Section 16.3, any legal action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought in the state courts located in California in the county where Client maintains its principal place of business, or the federal courts of the Northern District of California, and each Party irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts.

16.3 Optional Mediation. Before filing suit (except for claims for injunctive relief relating to confidentiality or intellectual property), either Party may request non-binding mediation administered by a mutually agreed mediator in California. The Parties shall share mediation fees equally, and each shall bear its own attorneys’ fees and costs for mediation.

clauses.title: Injunctive Relief
clauses.content: Contractor acknowledges that unauthorized use or disclosure of Client Confidential Information or unauthorized use of Client intellectual property may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages may be inadequate. Accordingly, Client may seek injunctive or other equitable relief, in addition to any other remedies available at law, without the necessity of posting a bond to the extent permitted by law.

clauses.title: General Provisions
clauses.content:
18.1 Assignment. Contractor may not assign or delegate this Agreement or any rights or obligations hereunder without Client’s prior written consent. Client may assign this Agreement in connection with a merger, acquisition, corporate reorganization, or sale of substantially all assets, upon written notice to Contractor.

18.2 Subcontractors. Contractor shall not use subcontractors to perform Services without Client’s prior written consent. Contractor remains responsible for all acts and omissions of any approved subcontractors.

18.3 Severability. If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect, and the Parties shall negotiate in good faith a valid, enforceable substitute provision that most closely reflects the Parties’ original intent.

18.4 Waiver. No waiver of any breach shall be deemed a waiver of any other breach. A waiver must be in writing and signed by the waiving Party.

18.5 Entire Agreement. This Agreement, together with any signed change orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties regarding its subject matter and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements and understandings, whether written or oral.

18.6 Amendment. Any amendment or modification to this Agreement must be in writing and signed by both Parties (email or electronic signature acceptable).

18.7 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original. Signatures delivered electronically (including via PDF or e-signature platform) shall be deemed effective.

18.8 Interpretation. Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation. The words “include” and “including” mean “include without limitation.”

contract.title: Consulting Agreement (California)
contract.preamble: This Consulting Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of January 20, 2025 (the "Effective Date"), by and between Growth Enterprises, with its principal place of business in California ("Client"), and JM Consulting, a consulting business operated by James Martinez ("Consultant"). Client and Consultant may be referred to herein individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties." Rachel Green, VP of Strategy, is authorized to execute this Agreement on behalf of Client, and James Martinez, Strategy Consultant, is authorized to execute this Agreement on behalf of Consultant.
contract.recitals: WHEREAS, Client desires to engage Consultant to provide strategy consulting services related to market expansion into Southeast Asian markets; WHEREAS, Consultant represents that Consultant has the expertise to perform such services; WHEREAS, the Parties desire to set forth the terms and conditions governing Consultant’s services, compensation, and related matters; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises set forth herein, the Parties agree as follows:

clauses.title: Engagement; Scope of Services
clauses.content:
1.1 Engagement. Client hereby engages Consultant, and Consultant hereby accepts such engagement, to perform the consulting services described in this Agreement.

1.2 Scope of Services. Consultant shall provide market expansion strategy development for Southeast Asian markets, including (a) competitive analysis, (b) partner identification, and (c) go-to-market planning (collectively, the "Services").

1.3 Change Requests. Any material change to the Services, deliverables, schedule, or assumptions shall be set forth in a written change order or amendment signed by both Parties. Absent a signed writing, Consultant is not obligated to perform out-of-scope work.

clauses.title: Term; Schedule; Completion
clauses.content:
2.1 Term. The term of this Agreement begins on the Effective Date and ends on June 20, 2025 (the "End Date"), unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Agreement (the "Term").

2.2 Schedule and Availability. Consultant will use commercially reasonable efforts to perform the Services during the Term and to coordinate meetings and communications with Client personnel as reasonably required.

2.3 No Guarantee. Consultant shall provide advice, analysis, and recommendations; Client acknowledges that business outcomes are dependent on factors beyond Consultant’s control and are not guaranteed.

clauses.title: Deliverables; Acceptance
clauses.content:
3.1 Deliverables. Consultant shall use commercially reasonable efforts to produce the following deliverables (collectively, the "Deliverables"): (a) Market Analysis Report; (b) Partner Recommendation Deck; and (c) 90-Day Implementation Plan.

3.2 Client Responsibilities. Client shall timely provide access to relevant information, stakeholders, and systems reasonably required for performance, and shall promptly review Deliverables and provide feedback.

3.3 Acceptance Procedure. Unless otherwise agreed in writing, Client shall have five (5) business days after receipt of a Deliverable to notify Consultant in writing of any material nonconformity with the agreed scope (an "Acceptance Notice"). If Client does not provide an Acceptance Notice within such period, the Deliverable will be deemed accepted. If a timely Acceptance Notice is provided, Consultant shall use reasonable efforts to address the stated nonconformity within the scope.

clauses.title: Fees; Retainer; Hourly Charges; Invoicing
clauses.content:
4.1 Fees. Client shall pay Consultant (a) a retainer in the amount of $5,000 (the "Retainer"), and (b) hourly fees at a rate of $250 per hour (the "Hourly Rate") for time spent performing the Services.

4.2 Retainer Application. Unless otherwise stated on an invoice, the Retainer will be applied as a credit against the first invoiced fees. Any unused portion of the Retainer at the end of the Term will be refunded to Client within thirty (30) days after final reconciliation; any amounts due in excess of the Retainer remain payable by Client.

4.3 Maximum Hours Cap. Consultant shall not bill more than forty (40) hours in total under this Agreement (the "Maximum Hours") without Client’s prior written approval.

4.4 Invoicing. Consultant may invoice (a) the Retainer upon the Effective Date, and (b) Hourly Rate fees in arrears on a monthly basis (or more frequently if reasonably necessary), with reasonable detail describing dates and general categories of work performed.

4.5 Payment Terms. Client shall pay all undisputed invoice amounts within fifteen (15) days of Client’s receipt of the invoice.

4.6 Disputed Amounts. If Client disputes any portion of an invoice, Client shall notify Consultant in writing within ten (10) days of receipt of the invoice, specifying the basis for dispute. The Parties will work in good faith to resolve the dispute. Client shall timely pay all undisputed amounts.

4.7 Late Payments. Amounts not paid when due may accrue interest at the lesser of (a) one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month, or (b) the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, from the due date until paid.

4.8 Taxes. Consultant is responsible for all taxes arising from compensation paid under this Agreement, except taxes based on Client’s net income.

clauses.title: Expenses
clauses.content:
5.1 Pre-Approval Required. Client shall reimburse Consultant for reasonable, necessary, and documented out-of-pocket expenses incurred solely in connection with performing the Services (e.g., travel) only if pre-approved in writing by Client.

5.2 Documentation. Consultant shall provide reasonable supporting documentation for reimbursable expenses and may invoice approved expenses in accordance with Section 4.

clauses.title: Independent Contractor Status; No Employment Relationship; AB 5 / ABC Test Acknowledgment
clauses.content:
6.1 Independent Contractor. Consultant is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, joint venturer, or partner of Client. Consultant has no authority to bind Client in any manner.

6.2 Control of Work. Consultant retains the right to determine the method, details, and means of performing the Services, subject to the scope, deliverables, deadlines, and specifications agreed by the Parties.

6.3 No Benefits. Consultant is not eligible for any Client employee benefits, including without limitation health insurance, retirement benefits, paid leave, overtime, or workers’ compensation coverage provided to employees.

6.4 Taxes and Withholding. Client will not withhold payroll taxes from payments to Consultant. Consultant is solely responsible for all federal, state, and local taxes, withholdings, and reporting obligations applicable to Consultant.

6.5 AB 5 / ABC Test. The Parties intend that Consultant perform the Services as an independent contractor consistent with applicable California law, including the standards commonly referred to as the "ABC test" (as applicable). Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as requiring Consultant to perform Services in a manner inconsistent with independent contractor status.

clauses.title: Confidentiality
clauses.content:
7.1 Definition. "Confidential Information" means all non-public information disclosed by or on behalf of Client to Consultant (whether in oral, written, electronic, or other form) that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure, including business plans, strategies, financial information, customer information, product information, pricing, and trade secrets.

7.2 Obligations. Consultant shall (a) use Confidential Information solely for the purpose of performing the Services; (b) not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without Client’s prior written consent; and (c) protect Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care Consultant uses to protect Consultant’s own confidential information of like importance, but no less than reasonable care.

7.3 Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that Consultant can demonstrate by competent evidence: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was lawfully known to Consultant without restriction before receipt from Client; (c) is lawfully received from a third party without breach of any obligation of confidentiality; or (d) is independently developed by Consultant without use of or reference to Confidential Information.

7.4 Compelled Disclosure. If Consultant is required by law, regulation, or court order to disclose Confidential Information, Consultant shall (to the extent legally permitted) provide Client prompt written notice and cooperate with Client’s efforts to seek protective treatment.

7.5 Duration. These confidentiality obligations commence on the Effective Date and continue for three (3) years after termination or expiration of the Agreement; however, trade secret information shall be protected for so long as it remains a trade secret under applicable law.

7.6 Injunctive Relief. Consultant acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or misuse of Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages may be inadequate, and Client may seek injunctive or equitable relief in addition to any other remedies.

clauses.title: Intellectual Property; Work Product; License Back
clauses.content:
8.1 Definitions. "Work Product" means all Deliverables and any other materials, analyses, reports, plans, presentations, notes, documents, and other work product created by Consultant specifically for Client in the course of performing the Services.

8.2 Ownership of Work Product. Subject to Section 8.3 (Pre-Existing Materials), upon full payment of all amounts due under this Agreement, Consultant hereby assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product, including all intellectual property rights therein, to the extent permitted by law.

8.3 Pre-Existing Materials. Consultant retains all rights in any pre-existing materials, templates, tools, methodologies, know-how, software, and generic materials owned or developed by Consultant outside of this Agreement ("Consultant Materials"). To the extent any Consultant Materials are incorporated into the Work Product, Consultant grants to Client a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use, reproduce, display, perform, and create derivative works of such Consultant Materials solely as embedded in or necessary to use the Work Product for Client’s internal business purposes.

8.4 Further Assurances. Consultant shall execute and deliver documents and take reasonable actions as Client may request to effectuate the assignment in this Section.

8.5 Moral Rights Waiver. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Consultant hereby waives and agrees not to assert any "moral rights" or similar rights in the Work Product.

clauses.title: Non-Competition; Non-Solicitation; California Compliance
clauses.content:
9.1 No Non-Compete in California. The Parties acknowledge that, except in limited statutory circumstances, covenants not to compete are generally void and unenforceable under California Business and Professions Code section 16600 and related law. Accordingly, notwithstanding any request to include a non-compete period of twelve (12) months, the Parties do not intend to create and this Agreement does not create any post-engagement non-competition restriction on Consultant.

9.2 Permitted Protection of Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement restricts Consultant from engaging in Consultant’s profession, trade, or business. However, Consultant remains bound by Section 7 (Confidentiality) and shall not misuse or disclose Client’s Confidential Information.

9.3 Non-Solicitation of Employees (Negotiable; May Be Unenforceable as Drafted). To the extent permitted by applicable law, during the Term and for twelve (12) months thereafter, Consultant shall not knowingly solicit for employment any employee of Client with whom Consultant had material contact through the Services. This Section shall be interpreted and enforced, if at all, to the maximum extent permitted under California law, and shall not restrict general solicitations not targeted at Client’s employees.

9.4 Severability/Blue Pencil. If any portion of this Section is determined to be void or unenforceable under applicable law, such portion shall be severed and the remainder shall remain in effect to the fullest extent permitted.

clauses.title: Representations and Warranties
clauses.content:
10.1 Mutual. Each Party represents and warrants that (a) it has full power and authority to enter into this Agreement and perform its obligations; and (b) the execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement does not violate any other agreement to which it is a party.

10.2 Consultant. Consultant represents and warrants that (a) Consultant will perform the Services in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with generally accepted industry standards; (b) to Consultant’s knowledge, the Work Product (excluding Client-provided materials and instructions) will not knowingly infringe any third-party intellectual property rights; and (c) Consultant will comply with applicable laws in performing the Services.

10.3 Disclaimer. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH IN THIS AGREEMENT, CONSULTANT MAKES NO OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR NON-INFRINGEMENT.

clauses.title: Indemnification
clauses.content:
11.1 Consultant Indemnity. Consultant shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Client and its officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any third-party claims, damages, liabilities, penalties, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of (a) Consultant’s gross negligence or willful misconduct; or (b) any third-party claim that the Work Product, as delivered by Consultant and used as permitted under this Agreement (excluding Client materials and modifications not made by Consultant), infringes or misappropriates such third party’s intellectual property rights.

11.2 Client Indemnity. Client shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Consultant and its officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any third-party claims, damages, liabilities, penalties, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of (a) Client’s gross negligence or willful misconduct; or (b) Client materials, instructions, or Client’s use of the Work Product outside the scope permitted by this Agreement.

11.3 Process. The indemnified Party shall (a) promptly notify the indemnifying Party of the claim (provided that failure to promptly notify shall only reduce obligations to the extent prejudiced); (b) allow the indemnifying Party to control the defense and settlement; and (c) reasonably cooperate at the indemnifying Party’s expense. No settlement imposing non-monetary obligations on the indemnified Party shall be entered without the indemnified Party’s prior written consent (not unreasonably withheld).

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses.content:
12.1 Cap. EXCEPT FOR (A) BREACH OF SECTION 7 (CONFIDENTIALITY), (B) A PARTY’S INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, OR (C) A PARTY’S GROSS NEGLIGENCE OR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY’S TOTAL LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT EXCEED THE TOTAL FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO CONSULTANT UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.

12.2 Exclusion of Certain Damages. EXCEPT FOR (A) BREACH OF SECTION 7 (CONFIDENTIALITY) OR (B) A PARTY’S GROSS NEGLIGENCE OR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR FOR LOST PROFITS, LOST REVENUE, OR LOSS OF GOODWILL, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT, OR OTHERWISE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

clauses.title: Termination; Suspension
clauses.content:
13.1 Termination for Convenience. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon ten (10) days’ prior written notice to the other Party.

13.2 Termination for Cause. Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other Party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within five (5) days after receiving written notice specifying the breach.

13.3 Effect of Termination. Upon termination or expiration: (a) Consultant shall cease performing Services; (b) Client shall pay Consultant for all Services performed and approved expenses incurred through the effective date of termination, subject to the Maximum Hours and Section 4; and (c) each Party shall return or destroy the other Party’s Confidential Information in accordance with Section 14.

13.4 Suspension. Client may suspend the Services upon written notice. During a suspension, Consultant shall pause work. If the suspension exceeds fifteen (15) days, either Party may terminate under Section 13.1.

clauses.title: Return of Property; Record Retention
clauses.content:
14.1 Return/Destruction. Upon Client’s written request or upon termination/expiration of this Agreement, Consultant shall promptly return or destroy Client’s Confidential Information in Consultant’s possession or control, except that Consultant may retain one archival copy solely for legal and compliance purposes, subject to continuing confidentiality obligations.

14.2 Records. Consultant may retain non-confidential business records necessary to document performance and invoices, subject to Section 7.

clauses.title: Notices
clauses.content:
15.1 Form. All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by confirmed email (with a copy sent by another method if requested), sent by nationally recognized overnight courier, or mailed by certified mail (return receipt requested), to the addresses designated by the Parties.

15.2 Addresses. The Parties shall provide notice addresses and notice emails upon execution of this Agreement, and may update them by notice in accordance with this Section.

clauses.title: Governing Law; Venue; Dispute Resolution
clauses.content:
16.1 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.

16.2 Venue. The Parties agree that any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state courts located in the County of Los Angeles, California, or, if federal jurisdiction exists, the United States District Court for the Central District of California, and each Party hereby submits to the personal jurisdiction of such courts.

16.3 Attorneys’ Fees. In any action or proceeding to enforce rights under this Agreement, the prevailing Party shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs.

clauses.title: Miscellaneous
clauses.content:
17.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement, including any exhibits or statements of work incorporated by reference, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties regarding its subject matter and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements and understandings.

17.2 Amendment; Waiver. Any amendment or modification must be in writing and signed by both Parties. Any waiver must be in writing and shall be effective only to the extent expressly stated.

17.3 Assignment. Neither Party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that Client may assign this Agreement in connection with a merger, acquisition, reorganization, or sale of substantially all assets, provided that the assignee assumes Client’s obligations.

17.4 Severability. If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

17.5 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which is deemed an original, and all of which together constitute one instrument. Signatures delivered electronically (including PDF or e-signature platform) shall be deemed original.

17.6 Headings. Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

17.7 Force Majeure. Neither Party shall be liable for delays or failures to perform due to causes beyond its reasonable control, except for payment obligations.
