contract.title: SAFE (Simple Agreement for Future Equity) — Valuation Cap and Discount (California)
contract.preamble: This Simple Agreement for Future Equity (this “SAFE”) is entered into as of January 25, 2025 (the “Effective Date”), by and between TechVenture Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of California (the “Company”), and Angel Investor Group LLC (the “Investor”). The individual executing this SAFE on behalf of the Company represents that they are the Company’s CEO and duly authorized to bind the Company, and the individual executing this SAFE on behalf of the Investor represents that they are the Investor’s Managing Partner and duly authorized to bind the Investor.

clauses.title: Defined Terms
clauses.content:
Capitalized terms used but not otherwise defined in this SAFE have the meanings set forth below:

(a) “Purchase Amount” means Two Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (US$250,000).

(b) “Company Capitalization” means, immediately prior to the applicable equity financing or liquidity event, the number of shares of Company capital stock outstanding on a fully diluted basis, including issued and outstanding shares, shares reserved for issuance under any equity incentive plan or similar arrangement, and all shares issuable upon conversion or exercise of any outstanding convertible securities (including other SAFEs, convertible notes, warrants, and similar rights), but excluding (i) shares reserved for issuance under any equity incentive plan that are not then approved by the Board, if any, solely to the extent such exclusion is required by the definitive financing documents, and (ii) shares issuable upon conversion of this SAFE.

(c) “Equity Financing” means a bona fide transaction or series of related transactions in which the Company issues and sells shares of its Preferred Stock (or other equity securities sold primarily for capital raising purposes) for aggregate gross cash proceeds to the Company of not less than US$1,000,000 (excluding conversion of this SAFE and excluding conversion of any other outstanding SAFEs or convertible instruments), unless a different minimum threshold is expressly approved by the Company’s Board of Directors (the “Board”).

(d) “Liquidity Event” means (i) a merger or consolidation of the Company with or into another entity, (ii) a sale, lease, exclusive license, or other disposition of all or substantially all of the Company’s assets, (iii) a change in control transaction in which the holders of the Company’s voting securities immediately prior to the transaction do not own a majority of the voting power of the surviving or acquiring entity immediately after the transaction, or (iv) any other transaction deemed a liquidity event under the Company’s organizational documents or definitive transaction agreements.

(e) “Dissolution Event” means (i) a voluntary or involuntary dissolution, liquidation, or winding up of the Company, (ii) an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or (iii) the appointment of a receiver for substantially all of the Company’s assets.

(f) “Valuation Cap” means US$5,000,000.

(g) “Discount Rate” means twenty percent (20%). For purposes of calculations, the “Discount Price” equals eighty percent (80%) of the price per share paid by the investors in the Equity Financing (i.e., applying a 20% discount).

(h) “Pro Rata Rights” means the right, but not the obligation, of Investor (or its permitted assignee) to purchase its pro rata share of equity securities issued by the Company in the Equity Financing, as further described in Section 7.

(i) “Preferred Stock” means the class or series of preferred stock issued by the Company in the Equity Financing.

(j) “Standard SAFE Forms; Construction.” This SAFE is intended to operate consistently with widely used SAFE market concepts; however, the specific terms of this SAFE control in all events.

clauses.title: Purchase Amount; Closing
clauses.content:
(a) Purchase Amount. Subject to the terms and conditions of this SAFE, Investor agrees to pay the Purchase Amount to the Company, and the Company agrees to accept the Purchase Amount.

(b) Closing and Payment Instructions. The Purchase Amount shall be paid to the Company on or after the Effective Date pursuant to wiring instructions or other payment instructions provided by the Company. Upon receipt by the Company of the Purchase Amount, this SAFE shall be deemed issued and outstanding.

(c) Use of Proceeds. The Company shall use the Purchase Amount for general corporate purposes, subject to the Company’s fiduciary duties and applicable law.

clauses.title: Conversion Upon Equity Financing
clauses.content:
(a) Automatic Conversion. Upon the closing of an Equity Financing, this SAFE will automatically convert into shares of the Preferred Stock.

(b) Conversion Amount. The number of shares of Preferred Stock issuable upon conversion of this SAFE shall equal the Purchase Amount divided by the applicable Conversion Price (as defined below).

(c) Conversion Price. The “Conversion Price” shall be the lower of:

(i) the Discount Price; and

(ii) the Cap Price.

(d) Discount Price. The “Discount Price” is the price per share paid by the investors purchasing Preferred Stock in the Equity Financing multiplied by (1 − Discount Rate). With a Discount Rate of 20%, the Discount Price is 80% of the Equity Financing price per share.

(e) Cap Price. The “Cap Price” is a price per share equal to (Valuation Cap) divided by the Company Capitalization.

(f) Fractional Shares. If the conversion results in a fractional share, the Company shall round down to the nearest whole share and pay Investor cash in lieu of the fractional share equal to the fractional portion multiplied by the applicable Conversion Price, unless the Equity Financing documents require a different treatment that is customary and non-discriminatory among SAFE holders.

(g) Mechanics; Issuance. The conversion shall occur immediately prior to the closing of the Equity Financing, and the Company shall issue the shares to Investor contemporaneously with the closing, subject to Investor providing customary representations and information (including accredited investor status if requested) and executing customary transaction documents applied uniformly to similarly situated investors converting SAFEs.

clauses.title: Liquidity Event
clauses.content:
(a) Election. If there is a Liquidity Event prior to the conversion of this SAFE in an Equity Financing, Investor shall be entitled, at Investor’s election, to receive either:

(i) Cash-Out Amount. A cash payment equal to the Purchase Amount (the “Cash-Out Amount”); or

(ii) Conversion Amount. A number of shares of the same class and series of capital stock issued to the Company’s stockholders in connection with the Liquidity Event (or, if not applicable, the class of common stock), equal to the Purchase Amount divided by the Liquidity Conversion Price.

(b) Liquidity Conversion Price. The “Liquidity Conversion Price” shall be the lower of:

(i) the Discount Price based on the price per share implied by the Liquidity Event (calculated by dividing the Company’s pre-transaction equity value by the Company Capitalization); and

(ii) the Cap Price.

(c) Timing and Notice. The Company shall provide Investor with written notice of the Liquidity Event at least ten (10) business days prior to consummation (or as much prior notice as reasonably practicable if less). Investor shall deliver written notice of its election under Section 4(a) at least two (2) business days prior to consummation (or as otherwise reasonably required to close).

(d) Treatment. If Investor elects to receive equity under Section 4(a)(ii), the Company shall take all actions necessary to issue such equity to Investor effective immediately prior to the consummation of the Liquidity Event. If Investor elects the Cash-Out Amount, such amount shall be paid at the closing of the Liquidity Event from the same funds otherwise distributable to the Company’s stockholders, subject to applicable law and the transaction agreements.

(e) Priority. The Cash-Out Amount shall be paid in preference to distributions to holders of Common Stock in the Liquidity Event to the extent legally permissible and consistent with the transaction structure, but junior to the claims of creditors and any senior securities as required by law or contract.

clauses.title: Dissolution Event
clauses.content:
(a) Payment Right. If there is a Dissolution Event prior to conversion of this SAFE, the Company shall pay Investor an amount equal to the Purchase Amount, due and payable immediately prior to or concurrently with the consummation of the Dissolution Event, to the extent legally available for distribution.

(b) Priority. The payment under this Section 5 shall be made after payment of the Company’s debts and liabilities, and shall have the same priority as the Company’s other obligations to holders of SAFEs, and senior to distributions to holders of Common Stock.

(c) Insufficient Funds. If the legally available assets are insufficient to pay the Purchase Amount in full, the Company shall pay Investor (and other SAFE holders) pro rata in proportion to their respective purchase amounts.

clauses.title: No Maturity Date; No Interest; No Repayment Except as Expressly Provided
clauses.content: This SAFE has no maturity date and bears no interest. Investor shall have no right to payment of the Purchase Amount or any other amount except as expressly provided in Sections 4 and 5. This SAFE is not a debt instrument and is not intended to create a debtor-creditor relationship except to the limited extent of the payment obligations expressly set forth herein in connection with a Liquidity Event or Dissolution Event.

clauses.title: Pro Rata Rights (Participation Right in Equity Financing)
clauses.content:
(a) Grant of Pro Rata Rights. Because Pro Rata Rights are true, the Company grants Investor Pro Rata Rights, subject to the terms of this Section 7.

(b) Scope. In connection with the first Equity Financing (and only the first Equity Financing unless otherwise agreed in writing), Investor shall have the right to purchase up to its pro rata share of the equity securities offered and sold by the Company for cash in such Equity Financing (excluding securities issued upon conversion of SAFEs, convertible notes, or other convertible instruments), at the same price and on the same terms as the other purchasers in such Equity Financing.

(c) Pro Rata Share. Investor’s “pro rata share” shall be the fraction equal to (i) the number of shares of Company capital stock issued or issuable upon conversion of this SAFE in the Equity Financing (calculated without giving effect to Investor’s exercise of this pro rata right), divided by (ii) the Company Capitalization immediately prior to the closing of the Equity Financing.

(d) Notice and Exercise. The Company shall provide Investor with notice of the Equity Financing and the terms thereof at least ten (10) business days prior to closing (or as much prior notice as is reasonably practicable). Investor must exercise its Pro Rata Rights by delivering a written notice of exercise and wiring the purchase funds at least two (2) business days prior to closing (or such other deadline as is reasonably required by the lead investor), failing which Investor will be deemed to have waived its Pro Rata Rights for that Equity Financing.

(e) Allocation; Oversubscription. If the Equity Financing is oversubscribed and allocations are reduced, Investor’s allocation under this Section 7 shall be reduced on the same basis as other participating investors with similar participation rights, as determined by the Company and the lead investor in good faith.

(f) Termination. The Pro Rata Rights under this Section 7 terminate upon the earlier of (i) the closing of the Equity Financing, (ii) a Liquidity Event, (iii) a Dissolution Event, or (iv) conversion of this SAFE in any Equity Financing.

(g) Transfer. Pro Rata Rights are transferable only together with this SAFE and only to a permitted transferee under Section 9.

clauses.title: Company Representations and Warranties
clauses.content:
The Company represents and warrants to Investor that:

(a) Organization; Power. The Company is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of the State of California, and has the requisite corporate power and authority to execute, deliver, and perform this SAFE.

(b) Authorization. All corporate actions required to authorize the execution, delivery, and performance of this SAFE have been taken. This SAFE constitutes a valid and binding obligation of the Company enforceable against the Company in accordance with its terms, subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, and similar laws and equitable principles.

(c) No Conflicts. The execution, delivery, and performance of this SAFE do not and will not conflict with or result in a breach of the Company’s organizational documents or any material agreement binding on the Company.

(d) Capitalization. The Company shall provide Investor with a true and correct capitalization summary upon reasonable request, subject to customary confidentiality limitations.

(e) Compliance with Law; Securities Laws. The Company will issue securities upon conversion of this SAFE in compliance with applicable federal and state securities laws, including California securities laws, and will use commercially reasonable efforts to qualify for an applicable exemption from registration.

clauses.title: Investor Representations and Warranties
clauses.content:
Investor represents and warrants to the Company that:

(a) Authority. Investor has full power and authority to enter into this SAFE. This SAFE constitutes a valid and binding obligation of Investor enforceable against Investor in accordance with its terms, subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, and similar laws and equitable principles.

(b) Investment Purpose. Investor is acquiring this SAFE for its own account for investment purposes and not with a view to distribution in violation of applicable securities laws.

(c) Accredited Investor. Investor is an “accredited investor” as defined in Regulation D promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and/or otherwise meets applicable requirements for an exemption from registration.

(d) Risk Acknowledgment. Investor acknowledges that an investment in the Company is speculative and involves a high degree of risk, that the SAFE and the underlying securities are illiquid, and that Investor may lose all of the Purchase Amount.

(e) Sophistication. Investor has such knowledge and experience in financial and business matters that it is capable of evaluating the merits and risks of this investment.

(f) No Reliance. Investor acknowledges it has had the opportunity to ask questions of and receive information from the Company and to consult its own advisors, and has not relied on any statements other than those expressly set forth in this SAFE.

clauses.title: Transfers; Permitted Transferees
clauses.content:
(a) General Restriction. Investor may not transfer or assign this SAFE, in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the Company, except as permitted under Section 10(b).

(b) Permitted Transfers. Investor may transfer this SAFE without the Company’s consent to (i) any affiliate of Investor, (ii) any entity under common control with Investor, or (iii) any partner, member, manager, or equity holder of Investor, provided that the transferee agrees in writing to be bound by this SAFE and the transfer complies with applicable securities laws.

(c) Compliance. Any transfer must be in compliance with applicable federal and state securities laws, and any purported transfer in violation of this Section 10 shall be null and void.

(d) Company Records. The Company may rely on its records to determine the holder of this SAFE for all purposes.

clauses.title: Most Favored Nation (MFN) (Optional)
clauses.content: Optional: If the Company issues any SAFE or other convertible instrument after the Effective Date with economic terms more favorable than those provided in this SAFE (excluding differences in valuation cap, discount, or pro rata rights unless expressly included), then upon written notice by Investor within thirty (30) days of learning of such issuance, the Company shall amend this SAFE to include such more favorable terms. This Section 11 is optional and shall apply only if checked/affirmatively agreed in writing by the parties; otherwise, it is of no force or effect.

clauses.title: Information Rights (Optional)
clauses.content: Optional: Until conversion or termination of this SAFE, upon reasonable prior written request and subject to confidentiality obligations and applicable law, the Company shall provide Investor (i) annual unaudited financial statements within one hundred twenty (120) days after fiscal year end, and (ii) quarterly unaudited financial statements within forty-five (45) days after quarter end. This Section 12 is optional and shall apply only if checked/affirmatively agreed in writing by the parties; otherwise, it is of no force or effect.

clauses.title: No Stockholder Rights
clauses.content: This SAFE does not entitle Investor to any rights of a stockholder of the Company (including voting rights, dividend rights, or inspection rights) until and unless the SAFE converts into equity pursuant to its terms. Upon conversion, Investor shall have only those rights provided to holders of the applicable class and series of stock issued upon conversion, as set forth in the Company’s charter documents and the definitive financing documents.

clauses.title: Tax Matters
clauses.content: The parties intend to treat this SAFE for U.S. federal and applicable state tax purposes consistent with its characterization as a prepaid forward contract or similar equity-linked instrument, unless otherwise required by applicable law. Each party shall be responsible for its own tax liabilities arising from this SAFE and the transactions contemplated hereby, and each party is encouraged to consult its own tax advisor.

clauses.title: Confidentiality (Optional)
clauses.content: Optional: Except as required by law or to a party’s attorneys, accountants, or other professional advisors who are bound by duties of confidentiality, each party shall keep the existence and terms of this SAFE confidential and shall not disclose them without the other party’s prior written consent. This Section 15 is optional and shall apply only if checked/affirmatively agreed in writing by the parties; otherwise, it is of no force or effect.

clauses.title: Compliance with California Law; No Non-Compete
clauses.content: Nothing in this SAFE shall be construed to restrict any person’s lawful profession, trade, or business in violation of California Business and Professions Code Section 16600. This SAFE contains no non-competition obligation and no term shall be enforced to the extent it would be void under applicable California law.

clauses.title: Independent Contractor Status; No Services (AB 5 Acknowledgment)
clauses.content: This SAFE relates solely to an investment by Investor and does not create any obligation for Investor or any person to perform services for the Company. Nothing herein creates an employment, agency, partnership, or independent contractor relationship. To the extent any services are separately agreed, such arrangement must be documented in a separate written agreement and must comply with applicable California law regarding worker classification (including, where applicable, the ABC test under California Labor Code Section 2775 et seq. (AB 5)).

clauses.title: Notices
clauses.content:
All notices and other communications under this SAFE must be in writing and will be deemed given (i) when delivered personally, (ii) when sent by confirmed email (provided that a copy is also sent by a nationally recognized overnight courier within one (1) business day), (iii) one (1) business day after being sent by nationally recognized overnight courier, or (iv) three (3) business days after being mailed by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, in each case to the addresses (or email addresses) set forth below or to such other address as a party may designate by notice in accordance with this Section.

If to the Company: TechVenture Inc., Attn: CEO, [Address], Email: [Email].

If to Investor: Angel Investor Group LLC, Attn: Managing Partner, [Address], Email: [Email].

clauses.title: Amendments; Waivers
clauses.content: Any term of this SAFE may be amended, waived, or modified only by a written instrument executed by the Company and Investor (or the then-holder of this SAFE). No waiver of any breach shall be deemed a waiver of any other or subsequent breach.

clauses.title: Assignment by Company
clauses.content: The Company may not assign this SAFE or any of its rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of Investor, except in connection with a merger, consolidation, or sale of substantially all of the Company’s assets where the surviving or acquiring entity assumes the Company’s obligations under this SAFE in writing.

clauses.title: Entire Agreement; Severability
clauses.content: This SAFE constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, whether written or oral. If any provision of this SAFE is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect, and the invalid or unenforceable provision shall be enforced to the maximum extent permitted by law consistent with the parties’ intent.

clauses.title: Counterparts; Electronic Signatures
clauses.content: This SAFE may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by electronic means (including PDF, DocuSign, or other electronic signature platforms) shall be deemed effective for all purposes to the fullest extent permitted by the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act (California Civil Code Section 1633.1 et seq.) and other applicable law.

clauses.title: Governing Law; Venue; Waiver of Jury Trial
clauses.content:
(a) Governing Law. This SAFE and any dispute arising out of or relating to this SAFE or the transactions contemplated hereby shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without giving effect to any conflict-of-laws principles that would result in the application of the laws of another jurisdiction.

(b) Venue; Jurisdiction. The parties agree that any legal action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this SAFE shall be brought exclusively in the state courts located in Santa Clara County, California, or, if federal jurisdiction exists, the United States District Court for the Northern District of California. Each party hereby irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction of such courts and waives any objection based on inconvenient forum.

(c) Waiver of Jury Trial. EACH PARTY HEREBY IRREVOCABLY WAIVES, TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, ANY RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY ACTION OR PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS SAFE.

contract.title: California Freelance Services Agreement (UX/UI Design) — E-commerce Platform Redesign
contract.preamble: This Freelance Services Agreement (the “Agreement”) is entered into as of 2025-02-01 (the “Effective Date”) by and between Creative Agency Co. (“Client”), by and through its Creative Director, and Jordan Taylor (“Freelancer”), a UX/UI Designer. Client and Freelancer may be referred to individually as a “Party” and collectively as the “Parties.”
contract.recitals:
WHEREAS, Client desires to engage Freelancer to provide professional UX/UI design services in connection with the project titled “E-commerce Platform Redesign,” consisting of a complete UX/UI redesign of an existing e-commerce platform including user research, wireframes, high-fidelity mockups, and design system documentation (the “Project”); and
WHEREAS, Freelancer represents that Freelancer has the skills, experience, and ability to perform such services; and
WHEREAS, the Parties desire to set forth the terms and conditions governing Freelancer’s services, deliverables, compensation, and related rights and obligations.
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises herein, the Parties agree as follows:

clauses.title: Engagement; Scope of Services
clauses.content:
1.1 Engagement. Client hereby retains Freelancer, and Freelancer accepts such engagement, to perform the services described in this Agreement (the “Services”) for the Project.

1.2 Scope. Freelancer shall perform the Services in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with generally accepted industry standards for UX/UI design. The Services include creation and delivery of the Deliverables described in Section 2 and such other tasks reasonably necessary to complete them.

1.3 Exclusions. Unless expressly included in this Agreement or in a written change order under Section 10, the Services do not include: front-end or back-end development, implementation, QA testing, content writing/copywriting, paid user recruitment costs, photography/videography, or ongoing post-launch support.

clauses.title: Deliverables; Milestones; Acceptance
clauses.content:
2.1 Deliverables and Milestone Schedule. Freelancer shall deliver the following deliverables (each, a “Deliverable”) on or before the stated due dates:
(a) User Research Report — due 2025-02-15 — milestone amount $2,000.
(b) Wireframes & User Flows — due 2025-03-01 — milestone amount $3,000.
(c) High-Fidelity Mockups — due 2025-03-31 — milestone amount $5,000.
(d) Design System Documentation — due 2025-04-30 — milestone amount $5,000.

2.2 Delivery Format. Deliverables shall be provided digitally in common industry formats. Unless otherwise agreed in writing, working files may include Figma links/files and exported PDF/PNG assets appropriate for review.

2.3 Review and Acceptance. Within five (5) business days after receipt of a Deliverable, Client shall either (i) accept the Deliverable in writing (including email) or (ii) provide written notice of specific nonconformities with this Agreement (a “Rejection Notice”). If Client does not provide a Rejection Notice within such period, the Deliverable will be deemed accepted.

2.4 Remedy for Rejection. If Client timely issues a Rejection Notice, Freelancer shall use commercially reasonable efforts to correct the identified nonconformities and resubmit the Deliverable. Client’s sole remedy for nonconforming Deliverables shall be correction/resubmission as provided herein, subject to Client’s timely cooperation and feedback.

2.5 Project Deadline. The overall project deadline is 2025-04-30 (the “Deadline”), subject to adjustment for Client delays, change orders, or events of force majeure under Section 14.

clauses.title: Client Responsibilities; Cooperation
clauses.content:
3.1 Timely Inputs. Client shall provide in a timely manner the information, access, materials, brand guidelines, existing analytics (if any), stakeholder availability, and feedback reasonably required for Freelancer to perform the Services.

3.2 Single Point of Contact. Client shall designate a single authorized representative to provide instructions, feedback, and approvals on Client’s behalf.

3.3 Delays. If Client’s failure to provide required inputs, access, or feedback causes delay, Freelancer shall not be responsible for missed milestones or the Deadline, and the schedule shall be equitably extended. If such delay results in additional work, Freelancer may request a change order under Section 10.

clauses.title: Fees; Deposit; Payment Schedule
clauses.content:
4.1 Total Fee. Client shall pay Freelancer a total fee of $15,000 (the “Total Fee”) for the Services and Deliverables described in this Agreement, subject to approved change orders.

4.2 Deposit. Client shall pay a non-refundable deposit of $5,000 (the “Deposit”) due on or before the Effective Date. The Deposit will be credited toward the Total Fee.

4.3 Milestone Payments. The remaining $10,000 shall be paid on a milestone basis upon acceptance (or deemed acceptance) of the Deliverables as follows:
(a) User Research Report — $2,000;
(b) Wireframes & User Flows — $3,000;
(c) High-Fidelity Mockups — $5,000;
(d) Design System Documentation — $0 (already allocated as $5,000 in milestone), with the final milestone payment being $0 only if prior milestone payments plus Deposit equal Total Fee.

4.4 Payment Alignment. The Parties acknowledge the milestone amounts total $15,000. Client shall pay: (i) Deposit $5,000 upfront; and (ii) remaining milestone amounts totaling $10,000 as invoiced and due, allocated across Deliverables. Unless otherwise specified in an invoice, the Deposit will be applied first to the earliest milestone(s) until exhausted.

4.5 Invoicing and Due Date. Freelancer may invoice upon execution for the Deposit and upon acceptance (or deemed acceptance) of each Deliverable for the applicable milestone amount not already satisfied by the Deposit. Invoices are due net ten (10) calendar days from invoice date.

4.6 Late Fees. Past-due amounts shall accrue interest at the lesser of (i) 1.0% per month or (ii) the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, from the due date until paid.

4.7 Taxes. Client is responsible for all applicable sales/use taxes, if any, excluding taxes based on Freelancer’s income.

4.8 Payment Method. Payments shall be made via ACH, check, or other method agreed by the Parties in writing.

clauses.title: Revisions; Out-of-Scope Work
clauses.content:
5.1 Included Revisions. The Total Fee includes up to three (3) rounds of revisions per Deliverable (the “Revision Rounds”). A “round” means a consolidated set of requested changes delivered by Client at one time in response to a submitted Deliverable.

5.2 Additional Revisions. Revisions beyond the Revision Rounds, or changes inconsistent with previously approved direction, are out of scope and will be handled via a change order under Section 10.

5.3 Out-of-Scope Examples. Out-of-scope items may include, by way of example: substantial change in design direction after approval, additional pages/flows not contemplated by the Project description, additional workshops beyond those reasonably required, or new deliverables not listed in Section 2.

clauses.title: Intellectual Property; Work Product Ownership; License
clauses.content:
6.1 Definitions. “Work Product” means all deliverables, designs, documents, reports, wireframes, mockups, specifications, and other materials created by Freelancer specifically for Client under this Agreement.

6.2 Ownership Transfer Upon Payment. Subject to Client’s full and final payment of all amounts due under this Agreement (including any change orders), Freelancer hereby assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product, excluding Freelancer Background IP and Third-Party Materials (defined below).

6.3 Freelancer Background IP. “Freelancer Background IP” means pre-existing tools, templates, methodologies, know-how, styles, components, plugins, or materials developed or owned by Freelancer prior to or outside this Agreement. Freelancer Background IP is not assigned. To the extent any Freelancer Background IP is incorporated into the Work Product, Freelancer grants Client a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use, reproduce, display, and modify such incorporated Background IP solely as necessary to use the Work Product for Client’s internal business purposes and operation of Client’s e-commerce platform.

6.4 Third-Party Materials. “Third-Party Materials” means materials not owned by either Party (including fonts, stock assets, icon libraries, or UI kits) that may be embedded or referenced. Client’s use of Third-Party Materials is subject to the applicable third-party license terms, and Client is responsible for obtaining necessary licenses unless expressly stated otherwise in writing.

6.5 Portfolio Rights. Freelancer may display the Work Product (including excerpts and process artifacts) in Freelancer’s portfolio, website, and marketing materials after the Work Product is publicly launched by Client or six (6) months after final delivery (whichever occurs first), provided Freelancer does not disclose Client Confidential Information. Client may request reasonable redactions.

6.6 Moral Rights Waiver (To the Extent Permitted). To the extent permitted under applicable law, Freelancer waives and agrees not to assert any “moral rights” in the Work Product against Client or Client’s successors/assigns.

clauses.title: Confidentiality
clauses.content:
7.1 Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by a Party (“Disclosing Party”) to the other Party (“Receiving Party”) that is designated confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information, including business plans, customer data, product information, designs, research, analytics, and credentials.

7.2 Obligations. Receiving Party shall: (i) use Confidential Information solely to perform or receive benefits under this Agreement; (ii) not disclose Confidential Information to any third party except to its employees/contractors with a need to know and bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Agreement; and (iii) protect Confidential Information using reasonable care.

7.3 Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that: (i) is or becomes publicly available through no breach; (ii) was known by Receiving Party prior to disclosure; (iii) is independently developed without use of the Confidential Information; or (iv) is rightfully received from a third party without duty of confidentiality.

7.4 Compelled Disclosure. If Receiving Party is required by law or court order to disclose Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall (to the extent legally permitted) provide prompt notice and reasonably cooperate with Disclosing Party in seeking protective treatment.

7.5 Return/Destruction. Upon written request, Receiving Party shall return or destroy Confidential Information, except that one archival copy may be retained solely for legal/compliance purposes.

7.6 Duration. These confidentiality obligations will continue for three (3) years after termination of this Agreement; however, trade secrets shall be protected for so long as they remain trade secrets under applicable law.

clauses.title: Independent Contractor Status; No Employment Relationship (California AB5/ABC Test Acknowledgment)
clauses.content:
8.1 Independent Contractor. The Parties intend that Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent (except as expressly authorized), partner, or joint venturer of Client.

8.2 Control. Freelancer retains the right to control and direct the manner and means of performing the Services, subject to Client’s right to specify desired results, deadlines, and project requirements.

8.3 Outside Usual Course/Customarily Engaged. Freelancer represents that Freelancer is customarily engaged in an independently established trade/occupation/business as a UX/UI designer and offers such services to multiple clients, and that the Services are provided in a professional capacity.

8.4 No Benefits. Freelancer is not entitled to Client-provided benefits, including without limitation health insurance, paid time off, retirement benefits, or unemployment insurance.

8.5 Taxes and Withholdings. Freelancer is solely responsible for all taxes, withholdings, and filings arising from compensation paid under this Agreement. Client will not withhold payroll taxes.

8.6 No Authority. Freelancer has no authority to bind Client or incur obligations on Client’s behalf unless expressly authorized in a writing signed by Client.

8.7 Compliance. Each Party shall comply with applicable laws, including California’s worker classification requirements. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to misclassify Freelancer, and the Parties will cooperate in good faith to adjust working arrangements if necessary to maintain compliance.

clauses.title: Tools; Accounts; Data Security
clauses.content:
9.1 Tools. Freelancer may use Freelancer’s own tools and subscriptions to perform the Services unless otherwise agreed in writing.

9.2 Client Systems Access. If Client provides access to systems, accounts, or data, Client shall provide access limited to what is necessary. Freelancer shall comply with Client’s reasonable security policies provided in writing.

9.3 Credentials. Each Party shall use reasonable measures to protect credentials and shall promptly notify the other of any known or suspected unauthorized access related to the Project.

9.4 Personal Data. To the extent Client provides personal data (if any) for research or analytics, Client represents it has the right to provide such data and that it has provided any required notices/consents. Freelancer will use such data only for the Project and will apply reasonable safeguards.

clauses.title: Changes; Change Orders
clauses.content:
10.1 Change Requests. Either Party may propose changes to the scope, Deliverables, schedule, assumptions, or requirements.

10.2 Change Order Requirement. Any change to scope, fees, or schedule must be documented in a written change order signed (including by email confirmation) by both Parties describing (i) the change, (ii) impact on fees, and (iii) impact on schedule.

10.3 Rates for Additional Work. Unless otherwise agreed in a change order, additional work will be billed at $150/hour in 0.1-hour increments.

clauses.title: Representations; Warranties; Disclaimer
clauses.content:
11.1 Mutual Authority. Each Party represents it has the full right, power, and authority to enter into and perform this Agreement.

11.2 Freelancer Warranty. Freelancer warrants that the Work Product will be original to Freelancer except for Third-Party Materials properly licensed or otherwise permitted for use, and that Freelancer will perform the Services in a professional and workmanlike manner.

11.3 Disclaimer. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED IN THIS AGREEMENT, FREELANCER MAKES NO OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR NON-INFRINGEMENT.

11.4 Client Materials. Client represents and warrants that any materials provided by Client (including trademarks, content, product information, and assets) do not infringe the rights of any third party, and that Client has the rights necessary to allow their use in the Project.

clauses.title: Indemnification
clauses.content:
12.1 By Freelancer. Freelancer shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Client from and against any third-party claims, damages, and reasonable attorneys’ fees finally awarded arising out of an allegation that the Work Product (excluding Client materials and Third-Party Materials) infringes a U.S. copyright or trademark, provided that Client (i) promptly notifies Freelancer in writing, (ii) allows Freelancer sole control of the defense/settlement, and (iii) reasonably cooperates.

12.2 By Client. Client shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Freelancer from and against any third-party claims, damages, and reasonable attorneys’ fees finally awarded arising out of (i) Client materials, instructions, or content, (ii) Client’s use of the Work Product in a manner not contemplated by this Agreement, or (iii) Client’s breach of this Agreement.

12.3 Mitigation. If a Deliverable is alleged to infringe, Freelancer may, at Freelancer’s option: (i) obtain the right for Client to continue using it; (ii) replace or modify it so it becomes non-infringing; or (iii) if neither (i) nor (ii) is commercially reasonable, refund the portion of fees paid for the specific infringing Deliverable, and this will be Client’s exclusive remedy for such infringement claim.

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses.content:
13.1 Cap. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, EACH PARTY’S TOTAL LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE AMOUNTS PAID OR PAYABLE TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT IN THE SIX (6) MONTHS PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE CLAIM.

13.2 Exclusion of Consequential Damages. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR FOR LOSS OF PROFITS, REVENUE, DATA, OR GOODWILL, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

13.3 Exceptions. The limitations in this Section 13 do not apply to: (i) a Party’s indemnification obligations under Section 12; (ii) a Party’s breach of confidentiality under Section 7; or (iii) Client’s payment obligations under Section 4.

clauses.title: Force Majeure
clauses.content: Neither Party shall be liable for delay or failure to perform due to causes beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, natural disasters, labor disputes, war, terrorism, governmental actions, internet/service provider failures, or power outages, provided that the affected Party gives prompt notice and uses reasonable efforts to resume performance.

clauses.title: Term; Termination; Effect of Termination
clauses.content:
15.1 Term. This Agreement begins on the Effective Date and continues until completion of the Services and final delivery of the Deliverables, unless earlier terminated under this Section 15.

15.2 Termination for Convenience. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ten (10) calendar days’ written notice.

15.3 Termination for Cause. Either Party may terminate immediately upon written notice if the other Party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within five (5) business days after written notice of breach.

15.4 Payment Upon Termination. Upon termination, Client shall pay Freelancer for: (i) all Services performed and Deliverables completed through the effective termination date, (ii) authorized non-cancelable commitments, and (iii) if termination is for convenience by Client, a wind-down fee equal to ten percent (10%) of the unpaid portion of the Total Fee to cover scheduling/administrative costs; provided that total payments shall not exceed the Total Fee unless increased by change order.

15.5 Work-in-Progress. Upon request and subject to payment of amounts then due, Freelancer shall provide Client with work-in-progress files for which Client has paid.

15.6 Survival. Sections intended by their nature to survive will survive termination, including Sections 4 (to the extent amounts are owed), 6, 7, 11, 12, 13, 16, and 17.

clauses.title: Non-Solicitation; No Non-Compete
clauses.content:
16.1 No Non-Compete. The Parties acknowledge that, except for statutory exceptions, covenants not to compete are generally void in California (Business and Professions Code § 16600). Accordingly, nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to prohibit Freelancer from providing services to other clients, including competitors of Client.

16.2 Optional Non-Solicitation (Limited). For a period of twelve (12) months following termination, Freelancer shall not knowingly solicit for hire Client’s employees with whom Freelancer had material contact through the Project; provided, however, that this Section does not prohibit general solicitations not specifically targeted at such employees. The Parties intend this provision to be interpreted in a manner consistent with California law.

clauses.title: Dispute Resolution; Governing Law; Venue
clauses.content:
17.1 Good Faith Negotiation. The Parties will attempt in good faith to resolve any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement through informal discussions between authorized representatives.

17.2 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.

17.3 Venue; Jurisdiction. The Parties agree that any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in Los Angeles County, California, and each Party irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction of such courts.

17.4 Attorneys’ Fees. In any action to enforce this Agreement, the prevailing Party shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs.

clauses.title: Notices
clauses.content: All notices under this Agreement must be in writing and will be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by confirmed email, or sent by nationally recognized overnight courier, to the addresses/email addresses designated by the Parties in writing. Either Party may update its notice information by written notice to the other.

clauses.title: Miscellaneous
clauses.content:
19.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement, including any change orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements and understandings.

19.2 Amendments; Waiver. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both Parties. A waiver is effective only if in writing and applies only to the specific instance.

19.3 Severability. If any provision is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect, and the invalid provision shall be reformed to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable.

19.4 Assignment. Neither Party may assign this Agreement without the other Party’s prior written consent, except that Client may assign this Agreement in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all assets, provided the assignee assumes Client’s obligations.

19.5 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which is deemed an original. Signatures transmitted electronically (e.g., PDF, e-signature platform) are binding.

19.6 Headings. Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

19.7 Relationship of the Parties. The Parties are independent contracting parties. Nothing in this Agreement creates a partnership, joint venture, fiduciary relationship, or agency relationship (except as expressly stated).

19.8 Publicity. Client may not use Freelancer’s name or likeness for endorsements without Freelancer’s prior written consent. Freelancer’s portfolio rights are governed by Section 6.5.

contract.title: MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT
contract.preamble: This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of January 15, 2025 (the "Effective Date"), by and between TechStart Inc., a California corporation, with its principal place of business in California ("TechStart" or "First Party"), represented by Alice Johnson, CEO, and InnovateCo LLC, a California limited liability company, with its principal place of business in California ("InnovateCo" or "Second Party"), represented by Bob Smith, CTO. TechStart and InnovateCo are sometimes referred to herein individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."
contract.recitals:
WHEREAS, the Parties wish to explore a potential business relationship concerning the exploration of a potential partnership for AI-powered analytics platform development (the "Purpose"); and

WHEREAS, in connection with the Purpose, each Party may disclose to the other Party certain confidential and proprietary information; and

WHEREAS, the Parties desire to set forth the terms and conditions under which such confidential information will be disclosed, received, and protected;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

clauses.title: DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
clauses.content:
1.1 "Confidential Information" means any and all non-public, confidential, or proprietary information disclosed by either Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party"), whether disclosed orally, in writing, electronically, or by any other means, and whether or not marked as "confidential," including but not limited to: (a) trade secrets, inventions, ideas, processes, formulas, algorithms, software, source code, data, know-how, improvements, discoveries, developments, designs, and techniques; (b) information regarding plans for research, development, new products, marketing, and selling; (c) business plans, budgets, financial statements, contracts, prices, suppliers, and customers; (d) information regarding the skills and compensation of employees, contractors, or other agents of the Disclosing Party; and (e) the existence and terms of this Agreement.

1.2 Confidential Information shall not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) was rightfully in the Receiving Party's possession prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party, as evidenced by written records; (c) is rightfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a third party without restriction on disclosure and without breach of any obligation of confidentiality; or (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of or reference to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, as evidenced by written records.

clauses.title: OBLIGATIONS OF RECEIVING PARTY
clauses.content:
2.1 The Receiving Party agrees to: (a) hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third parties, except as expressly permitted herein; (c) use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose; (d) protect the Confidential Information using the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information of like kind, but in no event less than reasonable care; and (e) limit access to the Confidential Information to those of its employees, officers, directors, agents, consultants, and advisors (collectively, "Representatives") who have a need to know such information for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations no less restrictive than those contained herein.

2.2 The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.

2.3 The Receiving Party shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party in writing upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information and shall cooperate with the Disclosing Party to help regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent further unauthorized use or disclosure.

clauses.title: PERMITTED DISCLOSURES
clauses.content:
3.1 The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by law, regulation, or valid court order, provided that the Receiving Party: (a) gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice of such requirement prior to disclosure (to the extent legally permitted); (b) cooperates with the Disclosing Party, at the Disclosing Party's expense, in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy; and (c) discloses only that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed.

3.2 If a protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the Receiving Party shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information that its legal counsel advises is legally required to be disclosed and shall exercise reasonable efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to such Confidential Information.

clauses.title: OWNERSHIP AND NO LICENSE
clauses.content:
4.1 All Confidential Information shall remain the sole and exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights, by license or otherwise, to the Receiving Party in or to any Confidential Information, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Purpose.

4.2 No license or conveyance of any intellectual property rights is granted or implied by this Agreement. Neither Party acquires any intellectual property rights under this Agreement except the limited rights necessary to carry out the Purpose.

4.3 Neither Party is obligated under this Agreement to disclose any particular Confidential Information or to enter into any further agreement with the other Party.

clauses.title: TERM AND TERMINATION
clauses.content:
5.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for a period of two (2) years, unless earlier terminated by either Party upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other Party.

5.2 The obligations of confidentiality and non-use set forth in this Agreement shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement and shall continue for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure of the applicable Confidential Information, or for such longer period as may be required to protect trade secrets under applicable law.

5.3 Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon the Disclosing Party's written request, the Receiving Party shall promptly: (a) return to the Disclosing Party all tangible materials containing Confidential Information; or (b) destroy all such materials and provide written certification of such destruction, signed by an authorized officer of the Receiving Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain one (1) archival copy of the Confidential Information solely for purposes of compliance verification, subject to the continuing confidentiality obligations of this Agreement.

clauses.title: NON-SOLICITATION
clauses.content:
6.1 During the term of this Agreement and for a period of one (1) year following its termination or expiration, neither Party shall, directly or indirectly, solicit, recruit, hire, or attempt to solicit, recruit, or hire any employee, contractor, or consultant of the other Party with whom such Party had contact or about whom such Party received Confidential Information in connection with this Agreement or the Purpose.

6.2 This restriction shall not apply to: (a) general solicitations of employment not specifically directed toward employees, contractors, or consultants of the other Party, such as advertisements in newspapers, trade publications, or on internet job boards; or (b) any individual who responds to such general solicitation without any other solicitation or inducement from the hiring Party.

6.3 Nothing in this Section shall prevent either Party from hiring an individual who independently contacts the hiring Party seeking employment without any solicitation by the hiring Party.

clauses.title: NO WARRANTY
clauses.content:
7.1 ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS." NEITHER PARTY MAKES ANY WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING THE ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, OR PERFORMANCE OF ANY CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, OR ITS FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

7.2 Neither Party shall be liable to the other Party for any damages arising from the Receiving Party's use of or reliance upon any Confidential Information, except to the extent such damages result from a breach of this Agreement.

clauses.title: REMEDIES
clauses.content:
8.1 Each Party acknowledges that the Confidential Information of the other Party is unique and valuable, and that disclosure or use of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement will result in irreparable harm and significant injury to the Disclosing Party that may be difficult to ascertain and for which monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy.

8.2 Accordingly, each Party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief, without the necessity of posting a bond or proving actual damages, to enforce the obligations under this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies it may have at law or in equity.

8.3 The rights and remedies of the Parties under this Agreement are cumulative and not exclusive of any other rights or remedies to which either Party may be entitled.

clauses.title: GOVERNING LAW AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION
clauses.content:
9.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

9.2 Any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement, including the determination of the scope or applicability of this agreement to arbitrate, shall first be submitted to non-binding mediation. If mediation is unsuccessful, the dispute shall be resolved by binding arbitration administered by JAMS in accordance with its Comprehensive Arbitration Rules and Procedures.

9.3 The arbitration shall be conducted in San Francisco, California, before a single arbitrator selected in accordance with JAMS rules. The arbitrator's award shall be final and binding, and judgment upon the award may be entered in any court having jurisdiction thereof.

9.4 Notwithstanding the foregoing, either Party may seek injunctive or other equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction to prevent irreparable harm pending the conclusion of any arbitration.

9.5 Each Party irrevocably consents to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the state and federal courts located in San Francisco County, California, for any action arising out of or relating to this Agreement that is not subject to arbitration.

clauses.title: GENERAL PROVISIONS
clauses.content:
10.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, relating to such subject matter.

10.2 Amendment. This Agreement may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by both Parties.

10.3 Waiver. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the Party granting the waiver. The failure of either Party to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of such provision or the right to enforce it at a later time.

10.4 Severability. If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions shall not in any way be affected or impaired thereby, and such provision shall be reformed to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable while preserving the Parties' intent.

10.5 Assignment. Neither Party may assign or transfer this Agreement or any rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that either Party may assign this Agreement in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Section shall be void.

10.6 Notices. All notices, requests, demands, and other communications under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given: (a) when delivered personally; (b) upon receipt of confirmation if sent by email; (c) one (1) business day after deposit with a nationally recognized overnight courier; or (d) three (3) business days after mailing by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, to the addresses set forth above or to such other address as either Party may designate in writing.

10.7 Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Electronic signatures and PDF copies shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes.

10.8 Relationship of Parties. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create a partnership, joint venture, agency, or employment relationship between the Parties. Each Party is an independent contractor and has no authority to bind the other Party.

10.9 Interpretation. The headings in this Agreement are for convenience only and shall not affect the interpretation of this Agreement. The word "including" means "including without limitation." This Agreement shall be construed without regard to any presumption or rule requiring construction against the drafting Party.

Disclosing Party and Receiving Party may be referred to herein individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

clauses.title: DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
clauses.content:
1.1 "Confidential Information" means any and all non-public information, in any form or medium, whether written, oral, electronic, visual, or otherwise, that is disclosed by or on behalf of Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, either directly or indirectly, including but not limited to: (a) trade secrets, inventions, ideas, processes, formulas, algorithms, software, source code, data, programs, and other works of authorship; (b) business plans, financial information, projections, customer lists, supplier lists, pricing information, and marketing strategies; (c) product designs, specifications, prototypes, and technical documentation; (d) research and development activities; (e) personnel information and organizational structure; and (f) any other information that is designated as "confidential," "proprietary," or with a similar designation, or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure.

1.2 Confidential Information shall also include: (a) the existence and terms of this Agreement; (b) the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Parties concerning the Purpose; and (c) any analyses, compilations, studies, reports, or other documents prepared by Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or otherwise reflect or are generated from Confidential Information.

clauses.title: EXCLUSIONS FROM CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
clauses.content:
2.1 Confidential Information shall not include information that Receiving Party can demonstrate by competent written evidence: (a) was publicly known and generally available in the public domain prior to the time of disclosure by Disclosing Party; (b) becomes publicly known and generally available after disclosure by Disclosing Party through no action or inaction of Receiving Party or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement; (c) was already in the possession of Receiving Party at the time of disclosure by Disclosing Party, as shown by Receiving Party's files and records existing prior to the time of disclosure; (d) is obtained by Receiving Party from a third party who is lawfully in possession of such information and is not subject to a confidentiality obligation to Disclosing Party with respect to such information; or (e) is independently developed by Receiving Party without use of or reference to Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, as evidenced by contemporaneous written records.

2.2 Any combination of features or elements of Confidential Information shall not be deemed to be within the foregoing exclusions merely because individual features or elements are in the public domain or in Receiving Party's possession, unless the specific combination itself and its principles of operation are in the public domain or in Receiving Party's lawful possession.

clauses.title: OBLIGATIONS OF RECEIVING PARTY
clauses.content:
3.1 Non-Disclosure. Receiving Party shall: (a) hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not disclose, publish, or otherwise disseminate any Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party; and (c) protect the Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care that Receiving Party uses to protect its own confidential information of like kind, but in no event less than reasonable care.

3.2 Restricted Use. Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and shall not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose, including for Receiving Party's own benefit or the benefit of any third party, without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party.

3.3 Limited Disclosure to Representatives. Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information only to its officers, directors, employees, partners, members, agents, advisors, and consultants (collectively, "Representatives") who: (a) have a need to know such Confidential Information for the Purpose; (b) have been informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; and (c) are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those contained herein. Receiving Party shall be responsible and liable for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.

3.4 No Copies. Receiving Party shall not copy, reproduce, or duplicate any Confidential Information except as reasonably necessary for the Purpose, and all such copies shall be subject to the terms of this Agreement.

clauses.title: COMPELLED DISCLOSURE
clauses.content:
4.1 If Receiving Party or any of its Representatives becomes legally compelled by any court, governmental agency, or regulatory authority, or is otherwise required by applicable law, regulation, or legal process, to disclose any Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall, to the extent legally permitted: (a) provide Disclosing Party with prompt prior written notice of such requirement so that Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement; and (b) cooperate with Disclosing Party, at Disclosing Party's expense, in seeking such protective order or other remedy.

4.2 If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if Disclosing Party waives compliance with this Agreement, Receiving Party shall: (a) furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed, as advised by Receiving Party's legal counsel; and (b) use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that confidential treatment will be accorded to the Confidential Information so disclosed.

clauses.title: OWNERSHIP AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
clauses.content:
5.1 Ownership. All Confidential Information shall remain the sole and exclusive property of Disclosing Party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring upon Receiving Party any rights, license, or interest in or to any Confidential Information, or any patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret, or other intellectual property rights therein.

5.2 Return or Destruction. Upon the written request of Disclosing Party or upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, Receiving Party shall promptly: (a) return to Disclosing Party all documents, materials, and other tangible embodiments of Confidential Information in Receiving Party's possession or control; or (b) at Disclosing Party's option, destroy all such Confidential Information and certify such destruction in writing to Disclosing Party within ten (10) days of the request.

5.3 Retained Copies. Notwithstanding Section 5.2, Receiving Party may retain: (a) one (1) archival copy of the Confidential Information solely for purposes of determining its obligations under this Agreement; and (b) copies of Confidential Information that are automatically stored in backup systems or electronic archives made in the ordinary course of business, provided that such retained copies shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement for so long as they are retained.

clauses.title: NO WARRANTY; NO OBLIGATION
clauses.content:
6.1 No Warranty. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS." DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING THE ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, OR PERFORMANCE OF ANY CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, AND HEREBY EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.

6.2 No Obligation. Nothing in this Agreement shall obligate either Party to: (a) proceed with any transaction or business relationship between them; (b) enter into any further agreement; or (c) disclose any particular Confidential Information. Disclosing Party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to determine what Confidential Information, if any, to make available to Receiving Party.

clauses.title: TERM AND TERMINATION
clauses.content:
7.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date, unless earlier terminated in accordance with Section 7.2 (the "Term").

7.2 Termination. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other Party.

7.3 Survival. The obligations of Receiving Party with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed during the Term shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of two (2) years following the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information, or for so long as such Confidential Information remains a trade secret under applicable law, whichever is longer.

clauses.title: REMEDIES
clauses.content:
8.1 Irreparable Harm. Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement may cause Disclosing Party irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy.

8.2 Injunctive Relief. In addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity, Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief, specific performance, or other equitable remedies to prevent or remedy any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting any bond or other security.

8.3 Cumulative Remedies. The rights and remedies of Disclosing Party set forth in this Agreement are cumulative and in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other rights and remedies available at law or in equity.

clauses.title: INDEMNIFICATION
clauses.content: 9.1 Indemnification by Receiving Party. Receiving Party shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Disclosing Party and its officers, directors, employees, agents, successors, and assigns from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) any breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party or its Representatives; or (b) any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information by Receiving Party or its Representatives.

clauses.title: GOVERNING LAW AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION
clauses.content:
10.1 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

10.2 Jurisdiction and Venue. contract.title: INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AGREEMENT
contract.preamble: This Independent Contractor Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of February 1, 2025 (the "Effective Date"), by and between AppDev Corp, a California corporation ("Company" or "Client"), and Emma Wilson, an individual operating as an independent contractor ("Contractor"). Company and Contractor may be referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

clauses.title: ENGAGEMENT OF SERVICES
clauses.content:
1.1 Engagement. Company hereby engages Contractor, and Contractor hereby accepts such engagement, to perform the services described in this Agreement (the "Services") during the Term, subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein.

1.2 Description of Services. Contractor shall provide the following Services to Company: Full-stack development for mobile application including React Native frontend and Node.js backend with AWS integration (collectively, the "Services"). clauses.title: TERM AND TERMINATION
clauses.content:

2.2 Termination for Convenience. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, upon fourteen (14) days' prior written notice to the other Party.

2.3 Termination for Cause. Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other Party: (a) materially breaches any provision of this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within ten (10) days after receiving written notice thereof; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) engages in any unlawful business practice related to the Services.

2.4 Effect of Termination. Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement: (a) Contractor shall immediately cease performing the Services; (b) Contractor shall deliver to Company all Work Product (as defined below), whether complete or in progress; (c) Company shall pay Contractor for all Services satisfactorily performed through the date of termination within thirty (30) days of such termination; and (d) the provisions of Sections 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, and 10 shall survive termination.

clauses.title: COMPENSATION AND PAYMENT
clauses.content:

3.2 Payment Terms. Company shall pay all undisputed invoices within thirty (30) days of receipt of a proper invoice from Contractor. Invoices shall be submitted monthly and shall include a detailed description of the Services performed during the applicable period.

3.3 Expenses. Contractor shall be responsible for all expenses incurred in connection with the performance of the Services, unless otherwise agreed in writing by Company in advance. Any pre-approved expenses shall be reimbursed within thirty (30) days of submission of appropriate documentation.

3.4 Taxes. Contractor acknowledges and agrees that Contractor is solely responsible for the payment of all federal, state, and local taxes arising out of Contractor's activities under this Agreement, including but not limited to income taxes, self-employment taxes, and any other applicable taxes. Company shall not withhold any taxes from payments to Contractor and shall report all payments on IRS Form 1099-NEC as required by law.

3.5 No Benefits. Contractor acknowledges that Contractor is not entitled to any employee benefits from Company, including but not limited to health insurance, retirement benefits, paid time off, or workers' compensation coverage.

clauses.content:

This assignment includes all rights of attribution, integrity, disclosure, and withdrawal and any other rights that may be known as or referred to as "moral rights" (collectively, "Moral Rights"). To the extent that Moral Rights cannot be assigned under applicable law, Contractor hereby waives and agrees not to enforce any and all Moral Rights.

To the extent that any Work Product qualifies as a "work made for hire" under the United States Copyright Act (17 U.S.C. § 101), such Work Product shall be deemed a work made for hire and the copyright therein shall be owned by Company. To the extent that any Work Product does not qualify as a work made for hire, Contractor hereby assigns all copyright in such Work Product to Company.

4.4 Further Assurances. Contractor agrees to execute, acknowledge, and deliver any and all documents and to take any and all actions that may be necessary or desirable to effectuate the purposes of this Section 4, including but not limited to executing patent applications, copyright registrations, and assignments thereof. Contractor hereby irrevocably appoints Company as Contractor's attorney-in-fact for the purpose of executing such documents on Contractor's behalf if Contractor fails or refuses to do so within a reasonable time.

4.5 Pre-Existing Materials. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Contractor retains all right, title, and interest in and to any materials, software, tools, methodologies, or intellectual property that: (a) were developed by Contractor prior to the Effective Date; or (b) are developed by Contractor outside the scope of this Agreement and without the use of Company's Confidential Information (collectively, "Pre-Existing Materials").
