If Company consents to the incorporation of third-party materials, Contractor shall ensure that Company receives a license to such materials sufficient for Company's intended use of the Work Product.

clauses.title: CONFIDENTIALITY
clauses.content:
5.1 Definition of Confidential Information. "Confidential Information" means any and all non-public information, in any form or medium, disclosed by or on behalf of Company to Contractor, or otherwise obtained by Contractor in connection with the Services, including but not limited to: (a) business plans, strategies, and forecasts; (b) financial information and data; (c) customer and vendor lists and information; (d) product and service specifications, designs, and roadmaps; (e) software, source code, and technical documentation; (f) trade secrets; (g) marketing and sales information; and (h) any other information designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure.

5.2 Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of Contractor; (b) was rightfully in Contractor's possession prior to disclosure by Company, as evidenced by Contractor's written records; (c) is rightfully obtained by Contractor from a third party without restriction on disclosure; or (d) is independently developed by Contractor without use of or reference to Company's Confidential Information, as evidenced by Contractor's written records.

5.3 Obligations. Contractor agrees to: (a) hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party without Company's prior written consent; (c) use Confidential Information solely for the purpose of performing the Services; (d) protect Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care that Contractor uses to protect Contractor's own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care; and (e) limit access to Confidential Information to those who have a need to know and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as those contained herein.

5.4 Required Disclosure. If Contractor is required by law, regulation, or court order to disclose any Confidential Information, Contractor shall: (a) promptly notify Company in writing prior to such disclosure, to the extent legally permitted; (b) cooperate with Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy; and (c) disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed.

5.5 Return of Confidential Information. Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon Company's request, Contractor shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information and all copies thereof in Contractor's possession or control, and shall certify in writing that Contractor has done so.

5.6 Duration. The obligations set forth in this Section 5 shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement and shall continue for a period of five (5) years from the date of disclosure of the applicable Confidential Information, provided that obligations with respect to trade secrets shall continue for as long as such information remains a trade secret under applicable law.

clauses.title: INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR RELATIONSHIP
clauses.content:
6.1 Independent Contractor Status. The Parties expressly intend and agree that Contractor is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, joint venturer, or partner of Company. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create an employment relationship, agency, partnership, or joint venture between the Parties.

6.2 Control and Method of Work. Contractor shall have sole control over the manner and means of performing the Services, including the time, place, and method of performance. Company's interest is solely in the results of the Services. Contractor represents and warrants that Contractor satisfies all three prongs of this test.

6.4 No Authority to Bind. Contractor has no authority to bind Company or to assume or create any obligation, express or implied, on behalf of Company. Contractor shall not hold himself or herself out as having any such authority.

6.5 Equipment and Materials. Contractor shall provide all equipment, tools, and materials necessary to perform the Services at Contractor's own expense, unless otherwise agreed in writing by Company.

6.6 Insurance. Contractor shall maintain, at Contractor's own expense, adequate insurance coverage, including general liability insurance and professional liability (errors and omissions) insurance, with coverage limits appropriate for the nature of the Services. Upon Company's request, Contractor shall provide certificates of insurance evidencing such coverage.

clauses.title: REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES
clauses.content:
7.1 Contractor's Representations and Warranties. Contractor represents and warrants to Company that: (a) Contractor has the full right, power, and authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform Contractor's obligations hereunder; (b) the execution and performance of this Agreement will not violate any agreement or obligation by which Contractor is bound; (c) Contractor has the skills, qualifications, and experience necessary to perform the Services; (d) the Services and Work Product will be original, will not infringe upon or misappropriate any intellectual property rights of any third party, and will be free from material defects; (e) Contractor will perform the Services in compliance with all applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards; and (f) Contractor is customarily engaged in an independently established business providing services of the same nature as the Services.

Company represents and warrants to Contractor that: (a) Company has the full right, power, and authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform Company's obligations hereunder; and (b) the execution and performance of this Agreement will not violate any agreement or obligation by which Company is bound.

clauses.title: INDEMNIFICATION
clauses.content:

Contractor shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Company and its officers, directors, employees, agents, successors, and assigns from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) any breach of this Agreement by Contractor; (b) any negligent or wrongful act or omission of Contractor in connection with the performance of the Services; (c) any claim that the Services or Work Product infringe or misappropriate any intellectual property rights of any third party; or (d) any claim that Contractor is an employee of Company, including any claim for wages, benefits, or taxes.

8.2 Indemnification by Company. The indemnifying Party shall not settle any claim in a manner that adversely affects the indemnified Party without the indemnified Party's prior written consent.

clauses.title: LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

EXCEPT FOR LIABILITY ARISING FROM BREACHES OF SECTION 5 (CONFIDENTIALITY), SECTION 4 (INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS), OR SECTION 8 (INDEMNIFICATION), IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER PARTY FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, PUNITIVE, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF PROFITS, GOODWILL, USE, DATA, OR OTHER INTANGIBLE LOSSES, ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT, REGARDLESS OF WHETHER SUCH DAMAGES ARE BASED ON CONTRACT, TORT, STRICT LIABILITY, OR ANY OTHER THEORY, EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

9.2 Cap on Liability.    (a) Market Analysis Report;

Consultant retains all right, title, and interest in and to Pre-Existing Materials. To the extent any Pre-Existing Materials are incorporated into the Work Product, Consultant hereby grants to Client a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide, royalty-free, non-exclusive license to use, reproduce, modify, and distribute such Pre-Existing Materials solely as incorporated in the Work Product.

6.5 Moral Rights. To the extent permitted by applicable law, Consultant hereby waives all moral rights in the Work Product, including rights of attribution and integrity.

clauses.title: REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES
clauses.content:
7.1 Mutual Representations. Each Party represents and warrants to the other that: (a) it has full power and authority to enter into and perform this Agreement; (b) the execution and performance of this Agreement does not conflict with any other agreement to which it is a party; and (c) this Agreement constitutes a valid and binding obligation, enforceable in accordance with its terms.

7.2 Consultant Representations. Consultant represents and warrants that: (a) Consultant has the requisite skills, experience, and qualifications to perform the Services; (b) the Services will be performed in a professional manner consistent with industry standards; (c) the Work Product will be original and will not infringe upon or violate any intellectual property rights or other rights of any third party; (d) Consultant has the right to grant the licenses and assignments set forth herein; and (e) Consultant will comply with all applicable laws, regulations, and professional standards in performing the Services.

7.3 Disclaimer. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH IN THIS AGREEMENT, CONSULTANT MAKES NO OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

clauses.title: INDEMNIFICATION
clauses.content:
8.1 Indemnification by Consultant. Consultant shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client and its officers, directors, employees, agents, successors, and assigns (collectively, "Client Indemnitees") from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) any breach by Consultant of any representation, warranty, or obligation under this Agreement; (b) any negligent or wrongful act or omission of Consultant in connection with the performance of the Services; (c) any claim that the Work Product infringes or misappropriates any intellectual property rights of any third party; or (d) any violation of applicable law by Consultant.

8.2 Indemnification by Client. Client shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Consultant and its officers, directors, employees, agents, successors, and assigns from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) any breach by Client of any representation, warranty, or obligation under this Agreement; (b) any negligent or wrongful act or omission of Client; or (c) Client's use of the Work Product in a manner not authorized under this Agreement.

8.3 Indemnification Procedure. The indemnified Party shall: (a) provide prompt written notice of any claim to the indemnifying Party; (b) grant the indemnifying Party sole control of the defense and settlement of the claim; and (c) provide reasonable cooperation to the indemnifying Party at the indemnifying Party's expense. The indemnified Party may participate in the defense at its own expense.

clauses.title: LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
clauses.content:
9.1 Limitation of Damages. IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER PARTY FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, PUNITIVE, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF PROFITS, GOODWILL, USE, DATA, OR OTHER INTANGIBLE LOSSES, EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

9.2 Cap on Liability. EXCEPT FOR CLAIMS ARISING FROM A PARTY'S GROSS NEGLIGENCE, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS, OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, NEITHER PARTY'S TOTAL CUMULATIVE LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL EXCEED THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO CONSULTANT UNDER THIS AGREEMENT DURING THE TWELVE (12) MONTH PERIOD PRECEDING THE CLAIM.

9.3 Essential Basis. THE PARTIES ACKNOWLEDGE THAT THE LIMITATIONS SET FORTH IN THIS SECTION 9 ARE AN ESSENTIAL ELEMENT OF THE BARGAIN BETWEEN THE PARTIES AND THAT, IN THE ABSENCE OF SUCH LIMITATIONS, THE TERMS OF THIS AGREEMENT, INCLUDING THE FEES, WOULD BE SUBSTANTIALLY DIFFERENT.

clauses.title: NON-SOLICITATION
clauses.content:
10.1 Non-Solicitation of Employees. During the Term and for a period of twelve (12) months following the termination or expiration of this Agreement, neither Party shall, directly or indirectly, solicit, recruit, hire, or attempt to solicit, recruit, or hire any employee of the other Party with whom such Party had contact in connection with this Agreement, without the prior written consent of the other Party.

10.2 Non-Solicitation of Clients. During the Term and for a period of twelve (12) months following the termination or expiration of this Agreement, Consultant shall not, directly or indirectly, solicit or attempt to solicit business from any client or customer of Client with whom Consultant had contact or about whom Consultant received Confidential Information in connection with the performance of the Services, for the purpose of providing services that are competitive with the Services provided under this Agreement.

10.3 Exception. The restrictions in this Section 10 shall not apply to general advertising or solicitations not specifically targeted at the restricted individuals or entities.

clauses.title: NON-COMPETITION ACKNOWLEDGMENT
clauses.content:
11.1 California Law. The Parties acknowledge that this Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of California. Pursuant to California Business and Professions Code Section 16600, covenants not to compete are generally void and unenforceable in California, except in limited circumstances involving the sale of a business or dissolution of a partnership or limited liability company.

11.2 No Non-Competition Restriction. Notwithstanding any request or provision to the contrary, this Agreement does not contain any covenant or restriction that would prohibit Consultant from engaging in any lawful profession, trade, or business. Consultant is free to provide services to other clients, including clients that may be competitors of Client, subject only to Consultant's confidentiality and non-solicitation obligations under this Agreement.

11.3 Confidentiality Remains in Effect. For the avoidance of doubt, the absence of a non-competition restriction does not limit or modify Consultant's obligations under Section 5 (Confidentiality) or Section 6 (Intellectual Property) of this Agreement, which remain in full force and effect.

clauses.title: DISPUTE RESOLUTION
clauses.content:
12.1 Negotiation. In the event of any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement (a "Dispute"), the Parties shall first attempt to resolve the Dispute through good faith negotiations between the Parties' designated representatives. Either Party may initiate such negotiations by providing written notice to the other Party describing the nature of the Dispute.

12.2 Mediation. If the Parties are unable to resolve the Dispute through negotiation within thirty (30) days of the initial notice, either Party may submit the Dispute to mediation administered by JAMS or another mutually agreed-upon mediation service in San Francisco, California. The Parties shall share equally the costs of the mediator.

12.3 Arbitration. If the Dispute is not resolved through mediation within sixty (60) days of the initial notice, either Party may submit the Dispute to binding arbitration administered by JAMS in accordance with its Comprehensive Arbitration Rules and Procedures. The arbitration shall be conducted by a single arbitrator in San Francisco, California. The arbitrator's decision shall be final and binding, and judgment on the award may be entered in any court of competent jurisdiction.

12.4 Equitable Relief. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either Party may seek injunctive or other equitable relief from a court of competent jurisdiction to prevent irreparable harm pending the outcome of arbitration, particularly with respect to breaches of Sections 5 (Confidentiality) or 6 (Intellectual Property).

12.5 Attorneys' Fees. In any arbitration, litigation, or other proceeding to enforce this Agreement, the prevailing Party shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees and costs from the non-prevailing Party.

clauses.title: INSURANCE
clauses.content:
13.1 Consultant Insurance. During the Term of this Agreement, Consultant shall maintain, at Consultant's sole expense, the following insurance coverage:
   (a) Commercial General Liability Insurance with limits of not less than One Million Dollars ($1,000,000) per occurrence and Two Million Dollars ($2,000,000) in the aggregate;
   (b) Professional Liability (Errors and Omissions) Insurance with limits of not less than One Million Dollars ($1,000,000) per claim and in the aggregate; and
   (c) Workers' Compensation Insurance as required by applicable law (if Consultant has employees).

13.2 Certificates of Insurance. Upon Client's request, Consultant shall provide certificates of insurance evidencing the coverage required under this Section 13.

clauses.title: GENERAL PROVISIONS
clauses.content:
14.1 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

14.2 Venue. Subject to Section 12 (Dispute Resolution), any legal action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in San Francisco County, California, and each Party hereby consents to the personal jurisdiction of such courts.

14.3 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, between the Parties.

14.4 Amendments. This Agreement may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by both Parties.

14.5 Waiver. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the waiving Party. No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right or remedy shall operate as a waiver thereof.

14.6 Severability. If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions shall not be affected or impaired thereby, and such provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable while preserving the Parties' original intent.

14.7 Assignment. Neither Party may assign or transfer this Agreement or any rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that Client may assign this Agreement to a successor in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Section shall be void.

14.8 Notices. All notices required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed delivered: (a) upon personal delivery; (b) upon confirmed transmission by facsimile or email; (c) one (1) business day after deposit with a nationally recognized overnight courier; or (d) three (3) business days after mailing by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, to the addresses set forth in the signature block or such other address as a Party may designate in writing.

14.9 Force Majeure. Neither Party shall be liable for any failure or delay in performing its obligations under this Agreement if such failure or delay results from circumstances beyond the Party's reasonable control, including but not limited to acts of God, natural disasters, war, terrorism, riots, embargoes, acts of civil or military authorities, fire, floods, epidemics, or strikes.

14.10 Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Electronic signatures shall be deemed valid and binding.

14.11 Headings. The section headings in this Agreement are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the interpretation of this Agreement.

14.12 Construction. This Agreement shall be construed fairly as to both Parties and not in favor of or against either Party, regardless of which Party drafted the Agreement.

contract.title: SIMPLE AGREEMENT FOR FUTURE EQUITY (SAFE)
contract.preamble: This Simple Agreement for Future Equity (this "SAFE") is entered into as of January 25, 2025 (the "Effective Date"), by and between TechVenture Inc., a Delaware corporation (the "Company"), and Angel Investor Group LLC, a limited liability company (the "Investor").

clauses.title: DEFINITIONS
clauses.content:
1.1 "Capital Stock" means the capital stock of the Company, including, without limitation, the "Common Stock" and the "Preferred Stock."

1.2 "Change of Control" means (i) a transaction or series of related transactions in which any "person" or "group" (within the meaning of Sections 13(d) and 14(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended), becomes the "beneficial owner" (as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended), directly or indirectly, of more than 50% of the outstanding voting securities of the Company having the right to vote for the election of members of the Company's board of directors; (ii) any reorganization, merger, or consolidation of the Company, other than a transaction or series of related transactions in which the holders of the voting securities of the Company outstanding immediately prior to such transaction or series of related transactions retain, immediately after such transaction or series of related transactions, at least a majority of the total voting power represented by the outstanding voting securities of the Company or such other surviving or resulting entity; or (iii) a sale, lease, or other disposition of all or substantially all of the assets of the Company.

1.3 "Company Capitalization" means the sum, as of immediately prior to the Equity Financing, of: (i) all shares of Capital Stock (on an as-converted basis) issued and outstanding, assuming exercise or conversion of all outstanding vested and unvested options, warrants, and other convertible securities, but excluding (A) this SAFE, (B) all other SAFEs, and (C) convertible promissory notes; and (ii) all shares of Common Stock reserved and available for future grant under any equity incentive or similar plan of the Company, and/or any equity incentive or similar plan to be created or increased in connection with the Equity Financing.

1.4 "Discount Rate" means 80% (representing a 20% discount).

1.5 "Dissolution Event" means (i) a voluntary termination of operations; (ii) a general assignment for the benefit of the Company's creditors; or (iii) any other liquidation, dissolution, or winding up of the Company (excluding a Liquidity Event), whether voluntary or involuntary.

1.6 "Equity Financing" means a bona fide transaction or series of transactions with the principal purpose of raising capital, pursuant to which the Company issues and sells Preferred Stock at a fixed valuation, including but not limited to, a pre-money or post-money valuation.

1.7 "Investment Amount" means Two Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars ($250,000).

1.8 "Liquidity Capitalization" means the number, as of immediately prior to the Liquidity Event, of shares of Capital Stock (on an as-converted basis) outstanding, assuming exercise or conversion of all outstanding vested and unvested options, warrants, and other convertible securities, but excluding: (i) shares of Common Stock reserved and available for future grant under any equity incentive or similar plan; (ii) this SAFE; (iii) other SAFEs; and (iv) convertible promissory notes.

1.9 "Liquidity Event" means a Change of Control or an IPO.

1.10 "Liquidity Price" means the price per share equal to the Valuation Cap divided by the Liquidity Capitalization.

1.11 "Pro Rata Rights Agreement" means a written agreement between the Company and the Investor (and holders of other SAFEs, as appropriate) giving the Investor a right to purchase its pro rata share of private placements of securities by the Company occurring after the Equity Financing, subject to customary exceptions.

1.12 "SAFE Preferred Stock" means the shares of the series of Preferred Stock issued to the Investor in an Equity Financing, having the identical rights, privileges, preferences, and restrictions as the shares of Standard Preferred Stock, other than with respect to: (i) the per share liquidation preference and the initial conversion price for purposes of price-based anti-dilution protection, which shall equal the SAFE Price; and (ii) the basis for any dividend rights, which shall be based on the SAFE Price.

1.13 "SAFE Price" means the price per share equal to the lesser of: (i) the Valuation Cap divided by the Company Capitalization; or (ii) the price per share of the Standard Preferred Stock sold in the Equity Financing multiplied by the Discount Rate.

1.14 "Standard Preferred Stock" means the shares of the series of Preferred Stock issued to the investors investing new money in the Company in connection with the initial closing of the Equity Financing.

1.15 "Valuation Cap" means Five Million Dollars ($5,000,000).

clauses.title: INVESTMENT
clauses.content:
2.1 Purchase Amount. In exchange for the Investment Amount, the Investor shall have the right to receive from the Company SAFE Preferred Stock or other consideration as set forth in this SAFE.

2.2 Payment. The Investor shall pay the Investment Amount to the Company within five (5) business days of the Effective Date by wire transfer to an account designated in writing by the Company.

2.3 Use of Proceeds. The Company shall use the Investment Amount for general corporate purposes, including but not limited to product development, operations, and growth initiatives.

clauses.title: CONVERSION EVENTS
clauses.content:
3.1 Equity Financing. If there is an Equity Financing before the termination of this SAFE, on the initial closing of such Equity Financing, this SAFE will automatically convert into the number of shares of SAFE Preferred Stock equal to the Investment Amount divided by the SAFE Price.

3.2 Liquidity Event. If there is a Liquidity Event before the termination of this SAFE and before an Equity Financing, the Investor will, at its option, either: (i) receive a cash payment equal to the Investment Amount; or (ii) automatically receive from the Company a number of shares of Common Stock equal to the Investment Amount divided by the Liquidity Price. If the Investor elects to receive shares of Common Stock, the Company shall take all actions necessary to ensure that such shares are issued to the Investor free and clear of all liens and encumbrances.

3.3 Dissolution Event. If there is a Dissolution Event before the termination of this SAFE, the Investor will automatically be entitled to receive a portion of the Remaining Assets equal to the Investment Amount, due and payable to the Investor immediately prior to, or concurrent with, the consummation of the Dissolution Event. The "Remaining Assets" means the cash and other assets of the Company available for distribution to the Company's stockholders after payment of all creditors and holders of liquidation preferences senior to the Investor.

3.4 Priority. In a Liquidity Event or Dissolution Event, this SAFE is intended to operate like standard non-participating Preferred Stock. The Investor's right to receive the Investment Amount shall be senior to the rights of holders of Common Stock, but junior to the rights of creditors and holders of any series of Preferred Stock issued prior to the Equity Financing.

clauses.title: PRO RATA RIGHTS
clauses.content:
4.1 Grant of Pro Rata Rights. The Investor shall have the right to purchase its pro rata share of any private placement of securities by the Company occurring after the Equity Financing. "Pro rata share" for purposes of this provision shall mean the ratio of (a) the number of shares of Capital Stock owned by the Investor immediately prior to the issuance of new securities in such private placement, assuming full conversion of this SAFE, to (b) the total number of shares of Capital Stock outstanding immediately prior to such issuance, assuming exercise or conversion of all outstanding vested and unvested options, warrants, and other convertible securities.

4.2 Pro Rata Rights Agreement. The Company shall enter into a Pro Rata Rights Agreement with the Investor upon the Equity Financing, which shall set forth the terms and conditions of the pro rata rights granted hereunder, subject to customary exceptions for (i) issuances to employees, directors, and consultants pursuant to equity compensation plans approved by the Board of Directors; (ii) issuances pursuant to the conversion of convertible securities; (iii) issuances in connection with strategic transactions; and (iv) issuances to banks or equipment lessors.

4.3 Notice. The Company shall provide the Investor with at least ten (10) business days' prior written notice of any proposed private placement of securities, which notice shall include the material terms and conditions of such offering, including the price per share and the number of shares to be sold.

clauses.title: REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES OF THE COMPANY
clauses.content:
5.1 Organization and Standing. The Company is a corporation duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of the State of Delaware, with full corporate power and authority to own its properties and conduct its business as presently conducted.

5.2 Authorization. All corporate action on the part of the Company necessary for the authorization, execution, and delivery of this SAFE, and the performance of all obligations of the Company hereunder, has been taken. This SAFE, when executed and delivered by the Company, shall constitute a valid and legally binding obligation of the Company, enforceable against the Company in accordance with its terms, except as limited by applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium, fraudulent conveyance, or other laws of general application relating to or affecting the enforcement of creditors' rights generally.

5.3 Compliance with Laws. The Company is in compliance in all material respects with all applicable laws, rules, and regulations, including, without limitation, all applicable federal, state, and local tax laws and regulations.

5.4 Capitalization. The Company's capitalization table, as provided to the Investor, is true, complete, and accurate as of the Effective Date.

5.5 No Conflicts. The execution, delivery, and performance of this SAFE and the consummation of the transactions contemplated hereby will not result in any violation or default of any provision of the Company's Certificate of Incorporation or Bylaws, or any instrument, judgment, order, writ, or decree to which the Company is a party or by which it is bound.

clauses.title: REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES OF THE INVESTOR
clauses.content:
6.1 Authorization. The Investor has full power and authority to enter into this SAFE. This SAFE, when executed and delivered by the Investor, will constitute a valid and legally binding obligation of the Investor, enforceable in accordance with its terms.

6.2 Accredited Investor. The Investor is an "accredited investor" as defined in Rule 501(a) of Regulation D promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Securities Act"). The Investor has such knowledge and experience in financial and business matters that the Investor is capable of evaluating the merits and risks of such investment, is able to incur a complete loss of such investment without impairing the Investor's financial condition, and is able to bear the economic risk of such investment for an indefinite period of time.

6.3 Investment Intent. The Investor is acquiring this SAFE and the securities issuable upon conversion hereof for investment for the Investor's own account, not as a nominee or agent, and not with a view to, or for resale in connection with, the distribution thereof, and the Investor has no present intention of selling, granting any participation in, or otherwise distributing the same.

6.4 Restricted Securities. The Investor understands that this SAFE and the securities issuable upon conversion hereof have not been, and will not be, registered under the Securities Act, by reason of a specific exemption from the registration provisions of the Securities Act which depends upon, among other things, the bona fide nature of the investment intent and the accuracy of the Investor's representations as expressed herein.

6.5 No General Solicitation. The Investor is not purchasing this SAFE as a result of any advertisement, article, notice, or other communication regarding the securities published in any newspaper, magazine, or similar media or broadcast over television or radio or presented at any seminar or any other general solicitation or general advertisement.

6.6 Due Diligence. The Investor has had an opportunity to ask questions of and receive answers from the Company regarding the terms and conditions of this SAFE and the business, properties, prospects, and financial condition of the Company.

clauses.title: INFORMATION RIGHTS
clauses.content:
7.1 Annual Financial Statements. The Company shall deliver to the Investor, within one hundred twenty (120) days after the end of each fiscal year, unaudited annual financial statements of the Company, including a balance sheet, income statement, and statement of cash flows.

7.2 Quarterly Updates. The Company shall provide the Investor with quarterly written updates regarding the Company's business, operations, and financial condition within forty-five (45) days after the end of each fiscal quarter.

7.3 Inspection Rights. Upon reasonable notice and during normal business hours, the Investor shall have the right to inspect the Company's books and records and to discuss the Company's affairs, finances, and accounts with the Company's officers.

7.4 Termination of Information Rights. The information rights granted under this Section 7 shall terminate upon the earlier of: (i) an Equity Financing (at which point the Investor shall be entitled to information rights as set forth in the applicable equity financing documents); (ii) a Liquidity Event; or (iii) a Dissolution Event.

clauses.title: MOST FAVORED NATION
clauses.content:
8.1 MFN Rights. If the Company issues any other SAFEs or convertible securities with terms more favorable to the investor thereunder than the terms of this SAFE, the Company shall promptly notify the Investor and, upon written request from the Investor delivered within thirty (30) days of such notice, this SAFE shall be amended to include such more favorable terms.

8.2 Exceptions. The foregoing shall not apply to: (i) any Equity Financing; (ii) any issuance of securities to employees, directors, or consultants pursuant to equity compensation plans approved by the Board of Directors; or (iii) any issuance of securities in connection with strategic transactions approved by the Board of Directors.

clauses.title: MISCELLANEOUS
clauses.content:
9.1 Governing Law. This SAFE shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law principles.

9.2 Venue. Any dispute arising out of or relating to this SAFE shall be resolved exclusively in the state or federal courts located in Santa Clara County, California. Each party hereby irrevocably consents to the jurisdiction of such courts and waives any objection to venue in such courts.

9.3 Successors and Assigns. The terms and conditions of this SAFE shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the respective successors and assigns of the parties. The Company may not assign this SAFE or any rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the Investor. The Investor may assign this SAFE or any rights hereunder to any affiliate of the Investor without the consent of the Company; provided that the Investor shall provide written notice to the Company of any such assignment. Any other assignment by the Investor shall require the prior written consent of the Company.

9.4 Amendment. This SAFE may be amended only by a written agreement signed by each of the parties hereto.

9.5 Waiver. No waiver of any provision of this SAFE shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the party against whom such waiver is sought to be enforced. No failure or delay by any party in exercising any right hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof.

9.6 Severability. If any provision of this SAFE is held to be invalid or unenforceable, such provision shall be struck and the remaining provisions shall be enforced to the fullest extent under law.

9.7 Entire Agreement. This SAFE constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings, both oral and written, between the parties with respect to such subject matter.

9.8 Notices. All notices required or permitted under this SAFE shall be in writing and shall be deemed delivered when delivered personally, or three (3) business days after being sent by registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, or one (1) business day after being sent by overnight courier, or upon confirmation of receipt if sent by email, to the addresses set forth on the signature page hereof or to such other address as either party may designate in writing.

9.9 Counterparts. This SAFE may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Electronic signatures shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes.

9.10 Attorney's Fees. In any action or proceeding to enforce the terms of this SAFE, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorney's fees and costs.

9.11 Tax Treatment. For U.S. federal and state income tax purposes, this SAFE is intended to be treated as a variable prepaid forward contract, and each party agrees to treat this SAFE consistent with the foregoing and to not take any contrary position on any tax return or in any other filing, unless required by applicable law.

contract.title: FREELANCE SERVICE AGREEMENT FOR UX/UI DESIGN SERVICES
contract.preamble: This Freelance Service Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of February 1, 2025 (the "Effective Date"), by and between Creative Agency Co., a company organized and existing under the laws of the State of California, with its principal place of business at _________________________ (hereinafter referred to as "Client"), and Jordan Taylor, an individual providing professional UX/UI design services, with a business address at _________________________ (hereinafter referred to as "Freelancer" or "Designer"). Client and Freelancer may be referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

clauses.title: DEFINITIONS
clauses.content:
1.1 "Confidential Information" means any and all non-public information, written or oral, disclosed by either Party to the other, including but not limited to business strategies, customer data, proprietary processes, trade secrets, and any information marked as confidential.

1.2 "Deliverables" means all work product, materials, documents, designs, wireframes, mockups, prototypes, design systems, and other tangible items to be delivered by Freelancer to Client as specified in this Agreement and Exhibit A.

1.3 "Intellectual Property Rights" means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, and any other proprietary rights recognized under the laws of any jurisdiction.

1.4 "Project" means the E-commerce Platform Redesign project as more fully described in Section 2 of this Agreement.

1.5 "Services" means the UX/UI design services to be performed by Freelancer as described in this Agreement.

1.6 "Work Product" means all inventions, discoveries, designs, developments, improvements, and works of authorship created by Freelancer in the course of performing the Services.

clauses.title: SCOPE OF SERVICES
clauses.content:
2.1 Project Description. Freelancer agrees to perform the following services for Client: Complete UX/UI redesign of existing e-commerce platform including user research, wireframes, high-fidelity mockups, and design system documentation (collectively, the "Services").

2.2 Deliverables. Freelancer shall provide the following Deliverables in accordance with the schedule set forth herein:

(a) User Research Report - Due: February 15, 2025
Comprehensive user research including user interviews, competitive analysis, persona development, and recommendations report.

(b) Wireframes & User Flows - Due: March 1, 2025
Complete wireframes for all key pages and user journey flows, including annotations and interaction specifications.

(c) High-Fidelity Mockups - Due: March 31, 2025
Pixel-perfect, high-fidelity design mockups for all screens across desktop, tablet, and mobile breakpoints.

(d) Design System Documentation - Due: April 30, 2025
Comprehensive design system including component library, style guide, typography, color palette, spacing guidelines, and implementation specifications.

2.3 Project Timeline. The Services shall commence on February 1, 2025 and shall be completed no later than April 30, 2025, subject to timely performance by Client of its obligations under this Agreement.

2.4 Change Orders. Any changes to the scope of Services or Deliverables must be documented in a written change order signed by both Parties, which shall specify any adjustments to fees, timeline, or other terms.

clauses.title: COMPENSATION AND PAYMENT
clauses.content:
3.1 Total Compensation. Client agrees to pay Freelancer a total fee of Fifteen Thousand Dollars ($15,000.00) for the complete performance of the Services described herein.

3.2 Deposit. Upon execution of this Agreement, Client shall pay Freelancer a non-refundable deposit of Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000.00) (the "Deposit"). The Deposit shall be credited against the final milestone payment.

3.3 Milestone Payment Schedule. The remaining compensation shall be paid according to the following milestone schedule:

(a) Upon approval of User Research Report: $2,000.00
(b) Upon approval of Wireframes & User Flows: $3,000.00
(c) Upon approval of High-Fidelity Mockups: $5,000.00
(d) Upon approval of Design System Documentation: $0.00 (covered by Deposit)

3.4 Invoice and Payment Terms. Freelancer shall submit an invoice upon completion of each milestone. Client shall pay each invoice within fifteen (15) calendar days of receipt. All payments shall be made via [wire transfer/ACH/check] to the account designated by Freelancer.

3.5 Late Payment. If any payment is not received within fifteen (15) days of the due date, Freelancer may, at Freelancer's discretion: (a) charge interest at the rate of one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month, or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less; (b) suspend work on the Project until payment is received; and/or (c) terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 10.

3.6 Expenses. Unless otherwise agreed in writing, Freelancer shall be responsible for all expenses incurred in connection with the performance of Services. Any pre-approved expenses shall be reimbursed by Client within fifteen (15) days of submission of receipts.

3.7 Taxes. Freelancer shall be solely responsible for all federal, state, and local taxes arising from compensation received under this Agreement. Client shall not withhold any taxes from payments to Freelancer.

clauses.title: REVISIONS AND APPROVAL PROCESS
clauses.content:
4.1 Revision Rounds. The compensation set forth in Section 3 includes up to three (3) rounds of revisions per Deliverable. A "revision round" is defined as a single consolidated set of feedback provided by Client within the timeframe specified in Section 4.2.

4.2 Feedback Timeline. Client shall provide consolidated feedback on each Deliverable within five (5) business days of submission. Failure to provide feedback within this period shall constitute approval of the Deliverable as submitted.

4.3 Consolidated Feedback. Client shall designate a single point of contact (the "Client Representative") who shall be responsible for collecting and consolidating all internal feedback before submitting to Freelancer. Contradictory or piecemeal feedback shall be counted as additional revision rounds.

4.4 Additional Revisions. Revisions requested beyond the three (3) included rounds shall be billed at Freelancer's standard hourly rate of One Hundred Fifty Dollars ($150.00) per hour, with a minimum of two (2) hours per additional revision round. Freelancer shall provide an estimate before commencing additional revision work.

4.5 Scope of Revisions. Revisions are limited to modifications of existing approved concepts. Requests for new concepts, significant departures from approved directions, or changes to the project scope shall be treated as change orders pursuant to Section 2.4.

4.6 Final Approval. Client shall provide written approval of each Deliverable upon satisfactory completion. Such approval shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed.

clauses.title: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
clauses.content:
5.1 Assignment of Rights. Upon receipt of full payment of all amounts due under this Agreement, Freelancer hereby assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the final approved Deliverables, including all Intellectual Property Rights therein. This assignment includes the right to modify, adapt, and create derivative works.

5.2 Pre-Existing Materials. Freelancer retains all rights to any pre-existing materials, tools, templates, frameworks, or methodologies owned by Freelancer prior to the Effective Date or developed independently of this Agreement ("Freelancer Materials"). To the extent any Freelancer Materials are incorporated into the Deliverables, Freelancer grants Client a non-exclusive, perpetual, royalty-free license to use such Freelancer Materials solely as part of the Deliverables.

5.3 Portfolio Rights. Freelancer retains the right to display the Deliverables, or portions thereof, in Freelancer's portfolio, website, and marketing materials, and to identify Client as a client. Client may request a reasonable delay of up to six (6) months before public display of the work.

5.4 Third-Party Materials. To the extent that any third-party materials (including stock images, fonts, or other licensed content) are incorporated into the Deliverables, Client shall be responsible for obtaining and maintaining any necessary licenses. Freelancer shall identify all third-party materials requiring separate licensing.

5.5 Rights Upon Termination. In the event of termination prior to completion, Client shall own only those Deliverables for which full payment has been made. All rights to unpaid work shall remain with Freelancer.

5.6 Moral Rights Waiver. To the extent permitted by applicable law, Freelancer waives any moral rights in the Deliverables, including rights of attribution and integrity.

clauses.title: CLIENT RESPONSIBILITIES
clauses.content:
6.1 Cooperation. Client shall cooperate with Freelancer and provide all information, materials, access, and approvals reasonably necessary for Freelancer to perform the Services in a timely manner.

6.2 Client Representative. Client shall designate a single representative authorized to provide direction, feedback, and approvals on behalf of Client. The initial Client Representative shall be the Creative Director. Client may change the designated representative upon written notice to Freelancer.

6.3 Materials and Access. Client shall provide Freelancer with:
(a) Access to the existing e-commerce platform and any relevant backend systems;
(b) Brand guidelines, style guides, and any existing design assets;
(c) Customer data and analytics as reasonably necessary for user research;
(d) Access to stakeholders and customers for research interviews; and
(e) Any other materials reasonably requested by Freelancer.

6.4 Timely Response. Client shall respond to Freelancer's reasonable inquiries and requests within two (2) business days. Delays in Client response may result in corresponding extensions to project deadlines.

6.5 Content and Legal Compliance. Client shall be solely responsible for ensuring that any content, materials, or instructions provided to Freelancer do not infringe upon the rights of any third party and comply with all applicable laws and regulations.

6.6 Impact of Client Delays. If Client fails to provide required materials, feedback, or approvals within the timeframes specified in this Agreement, Freelancer shall be entitled to extend the project timeline by a period equal to the delay.

clauses.title: INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR RELATIONSHIP
clauses.content:
7.1 Independent Contractor Status. Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, agent, or joint venturer of Client. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create an employment relationship, partnership, or joint venture between the Parties.

7.2 No Employee Benefits. Freelancer shall not be entitled to any employee benefits from Client, including but not limited to health insurance, retirement benefits, paid vacation, or workers' compensation. Freelancer acknowledges that Client will not withhold any taxes from payments made under this Agreement.

7.3 Control and Method. Freelancer shall have sole control over the manner and means of performing the Services, including the time, place, and method of performance. Client's sole interest is in the results to be achieved.

7.4 Tools and Equipment. Freelancer shall provide all tools, equipment, software, and materials necessary to perform the Services at Freelancer's own expense.

7.5 California ABC Test Compliance. The Parties acknowledge that Freelancer's engagement satisfies the requirements of California Labor Code Section 2750.3 (AB5) because: (a) Freelancer is free from control and direction in performing the Services; (b) the Services are outside the usual course of Client's business; and (c) Freelancer is customarily engaged in an independently established trade, occupation, or business of the same nature as the Services.

7.6 Other Clients. Freelancer is free to provide services to other clients during the term of this Agreement, provided such engagements do not interfere with Freelancer's obligations hereunder or create a conflict of interest.

7.7 Subcontractors. Freelancer may not engage subcontractors to perform any portion of the Services without Client's prior written consent. Any approved subcontractor shall be bound by confidentiality obligations no less protective than those contained in this Agreement.

clauses.title: CONFIDENTIALITY
clauses.content:
8.1 Confidential Information. During the term of this Agreement and thereafter, each Party agrees to maintain in strict confidence all Confidential Information of the other Party and not to disclose such information to any third party without the prior written consent of the disclosing Party.

8.2 Permitted Use. The receiving Party may use the disclosing Party's Confidential Information solely for the purpose of performing its obligations under this Agreement.

8.3 Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the receiving Party; (b) was known to the receiving Party prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed by the receiving Party without use of the disclosing Party's Confidential Information; or (d) is rightfully obtained from a third party without restriction on disclosure.

8.4 Required Disclosure. If either Party is required by law, regulation, or court order to disclose Confidential Information, the receiving Party shall provide prompt written notice to the disclosing Party to allow the disclosing Party to seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy.

8.5 Return of Materials. Upon termination of this Agreement or upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies thereof.

8.6 Duration. The obligations under this Section 8 shall survive termination of this Agreement and continue for a period of three (3) years following termination.

clauses.title: REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES
clauses.content:
9.1 Mutual Representations. Each Party represents and warrants that: (a) it has the full right, power, and authority to enter into this Agreement; (b) the execution and performance of this Agreement does not conflict with any other agreement to which it is a party; and (c) it will comply with all applicable laws and regulations in the performance of its obligations.

9.2 Freelancer Representations. Freelancer represents and warrants that: (a) Freelancer has the skills, experience, and qualifications necessary to perform the Services; (b) the Services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with industry standards; (c) the Deliverables will be original works and will not infringe upon the Intellectual Property Rights of any third party; (d) Freelancer will not incorporate any materials into the Deliverables that would require Client to pay royalties or licensing fees to any third party without Client's prior written approval; and (e) Freelancer has the right to assign the Intellectual Property Rights in the Deliverables as provided herein.

9.3 Client Representations. Client represents and warrants that: (a) Client owns or has the right to use all materials provided to Freelancer; (b) the use of such materials will not infringe upon the rights of any third party; and (c) Client will provide accurate and complete information as reasonably requested by Freelancer.

9.4 Disclaimer. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH IN THIS AGREEMENT, FREELANCER MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR NON-INFRINGEMENT.

clauses.title: TERM AND TERMINATION
clauses.content:
10.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue until the completion of the Services and payment of all amounts due, unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Section.

10.2 Termination for Convenience by Client. Client may terminate this Agreement at any time for any reason upon fifteen (15) days' prior written notice to Freelancer. In such event, Client shall pay Freelancer for: (a) all completed and approved Deliverables; (b) work in progress on a pro-rata basis calculated as a percentage of the current milestone completed; and (c) any pre-approved, non-refundable expenses incurred.

10.3 Termination for Convenience by Freelancer. Freelancer may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to Client. In such event, Freelancer shall complete any work in progress to a reasonable stopping point and deliver all completed work to Client.

10.4 Termination for Cause. Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other Party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fifteen (15) days after receiving written notice thereof; (b) becomes insolvent, files for bankruptcy, or makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors; or (c) is subject to any proceeding under bankruptcy or insolvency laws.

10.5 Effect of Termination. Upon termination: (a) Client shall pay all amounts due for work performed through the date of termination within fifteen (15) days; (b) each Party shall return or destroy the other Party's Confidential Information; (c) Freelancer shall deliver all completed work product for which payment has been made; and (d) the provisions of Sections 5, 8, 9, 11, 12, and 13 shall survive termination.

10.6 Non-Refundable Deposit. The Deposit paid pursuant to Section 3.2 is non-refundable except in the case of termination for cause by Client due to Freelancer's material breach.

clauses.title: LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
clauses.content:
11.1 Limitation. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, PUNITIVE, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF PROFITS, GOODWILL, USE, DATA, OR OTHER INTANGIBLE LOSSES, ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT, REGARDLESS OF WHETHER SUCH DAMAGES WERE FORESEEABLE OR WHETHER EITHER PARTY WAS ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

11.2 Cap on Liability. EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS OR INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY INFRINGEMENT, THE TOTAL CUMULATIVE LIABILITY OF EITHER PARTY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL AMOUNT PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.

11.3 Essential Purpose. THE PARTIES ACKNOWLEDGE THAT THE LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY IN THIS SECTION REFLECT A REASONABLE ALLOCATION OF RISK AND ARE A FUNDAMENTAL ELEMENT OF THE BASIS OF THE BARGAIN BETWEEN THE PARTIES. THE SERVICES WOULD NOT BE PROVIDED WITHOUT SUCH LIMITATIONS.

11.4 No Liability for Client Materials. Freelancer shall not be liable for any claims arising from Client's materials, content, or instructions, or for any delays caused by Client's failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement.

clauses.title: INDEMNIFICATION
clauses.content:
12.1 Indemnification by Freelancer. Freelancer shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client and its officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) any breach by Freelancer of any representation, warranty, or obligation under this Agreement; (b) any claim that the Deliverables infringe upon the Intellectual Property Rights of any third party; or (c) Freelancer's gross negligence or willful misconduct.

12.2 Indemnification by Client. Client shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Freelancer from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) any breach by Client of any representation, warranty, or obligation under this Agreement; (b) any claim arising from materials, content, or instructions provided by Client; (c) Client's use of the Deliverables in a manner not authorized by this Agreement; or (d) Client's gross negligence or willful misconduct.

12.3 Indemnification Procedure. The indemnified Party shall: (a) provide prompt written notice of any claim; (b) allow the indemnifying Party to control the defense and settlement of the claim; and (c) provide reasonable cooperation at the indemnifying Party's expense. The indemnifying Party shall not settle any claim in a manner that adversely affects the indemnified Party without the indemnified Party's prior written consent.

clauses.title: DISPUTE RESOLUTION
clauses.content:
13.1 Informal Resolution. The Parties agree to attempt to resolve any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement through good faith negotiations. Either Party may initiate informal dispute resolution by providing written notice describing the dispute. The Parties shall meet (in person or by video conference) within ten (10) business days of such notice to attempt to resolve the dispute.

13.2 Mediation. If the dispute is not resolved within thirty (30) days of the initial notice, either Party may initiate non-binding mediation by providing written notice to the other Party. The mediation shall be conducted by a mutually agreed-upon mediator in San Francisco, California, or remotely by mutual agreement. The costs of mediation shall be shared equally by the Parties.

13.3 Binding Arbitration. If the dispute is not resolved through mediation within sixty (60) days of the initial notice, either Party may initiate binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of JAMS. The arbitration shall be conducted by a single arbitrator in San Francisco, California. The arbitrator's decision shall be final and binding, and judgment on the award may be entered in any court of competent jurisdiction.

13.4 Exceptions. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either Party may seek injunctive or other equitable relief in a court of competent jurisdiction to prevent irreparable harm, including but not limited to breaches of confidentiality or Intellectual Property Rights.

13.5 Attorneys' Fees. In any dispute arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing Party shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees and costs from the non-prevailing Party.

clauses.title: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
clauses.content:
14.1 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

14.2 Venue. Subject to Section 13 (Dispute Resolution), any legal action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in San Francisco County, California, and each Party hereby consents to the personal jurisdiction of such courts.

14.3 Waiver of Jury Trial. TO THE EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES ITS RIGHT TO A JURY TRIAL IN ANY LEGAL PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT.

14.4 California Business and Professions Code. The Parties acknowledge that California Business and Professions Code Section 16600 renders non-compete agreements generally unenforceable in California. Accordingly, this Agreement contains no provisions restricting Freelancer's right to engage in any lawful profession, trade, or business.

clauses.title: FORCE MAJEURE
clauses.content:
15.1 Force Majeure Event. Neither Party shall be liable for any failure or delay in performing its obligations under this Agreement if such failure or delay results from a Force Majeure Event. A "Force Majeure Event" means any event beyond the reasonable control of the affected Party, including but not limited to: acts of God, natural disasters, war, terrorism, riots, civil unrest, government actions, epidemics, pandemics, power outages, telecommunications failures, or labor disputes.

15.2 Notice. The affected Party shall provide prompt written notice to the other Party of the Force Majeure Event, including a description of the event, its expected duration, and the steps being taken to mitigate its effects.

15.3 Extension of Time. Any deadline affected by a Force Majeure Event shall be extended by a period equal to the duration of the delay caused by such event.

15.4 Termination. If a Force Majeure Event continues for more than sixty (60) days, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice without liability, except for payment of amounts due for work completed prior to termination.

clauses.title: GENERAL PROVISIONS
clauses.content:
16.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement, together with any exhibits, schedules, and amendments hereto, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written.

16.2 Amendments. This Agreement may not be amended or modified except by a written instrument signed by both Parties.

16.3 Waiver. The failure of either Party to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of that Party's right to enforce that provision or any other provision in the future.

16.4 Severability. If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions shall not be affected or impaired thereby.

16.5 Assignment. Neither Party may assign or transfer this Agreement or any rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that Client may assign this Agreement to a successor in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets. Any attempted assignment in violation of this section shall be void.

16.6 Notices. All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed given when: (a) delivered personally; (b) sent by confirmed email; (c) one (1) business day after sending by overnight courier; or (d) three (3) business days after mailing by certified mail, return receipt requested. Notices shall be sent to the addresses set forth in this Agreement or to such other address as either Party may specify in writing.

16.7 Headings. The section headings in this Agreement are for convenience only and shall not affect the interpretation of this Agreement.

16.8 Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Electronic signatures shall be deemed valid and binding.

16.9 Relationship of Parties. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create a partnership, joint venture, employment, or agency relationship between the Parties.

16.10 No Third-Party Beneficiaries. This Agreement is for the sole benefit of the Parties and their permitted successors and assigns. Nothing in this Agreement shall confer any rights upon any third party.
